Pak Study MCQs · pages 751-800

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

Islamic banking started in Pakistan

Correct Answer: 1980
Show explanation
Explanation

On January 1, 1980, approximately 7,000 interest-free counters were opened at all the nationalized commercial banks, making Pakistan the first country in the Muslim world with Islamic banking.

2

Education Commission was created by Ayoub Khan in _____?

Correct Answer: 1959
Show explanation
Explanation

The Commission on National Education was appointed by a resolution adopted by the government on 30"' December 1958.

It was inaugurated by the President, Mohammad Ayub Khan on January 5, 1959.

3

In which year MAO School was upgraded to the status of college?

Correct Answer: 1877
Show explanation
Explanation

Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College was founded in 1875 by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan, initially as a primary school, with the intention of taking it to a college level institution, known as Muhammedan Anglo Oriental Collegiate School. Its given Urdu name was Madrasatul Uloom Musalmanan-e-Hind.

4

NEPRA was created when the Parliament of Pakistan passed the NEPRA Act______?

Correct Answer: 1997
Show explanation
Explanation

The National Electric Power Regulatory Authority is responsible for regulating the electricity supply in Pakistan. 

Founded: December 16, 1997

Headquarters: Islamabad 

5

The main HQ of tableeghi jamaat referred to as the Markaz is in ________?

Correct Answer: Delhi
Show explanation
Explanation

Tablighi Jamaat was started by Deobandi Islamic scholar Maulana Mohammad Ilyas Kandhalvi as a religious reform movement.

The work of Tablighi Jamaat is to give religious sermons especially to the followers of Islam.



6

The village Agricultural and Industrial Development (Village-Aid) program was initiated in_________?

Correct Answer: 1953
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan's industrial sector (in FY21) accounts for 28.11% of the GDP

7

Indemnity provision of FIA Act is given in section?

Correct Answer: 8
Show explanation
Explanation
8

Superintendence of Agency has been given in Section:

Correct Answer: 4
Show explanation
Explanation

Section 4(0) defines the Superintendence and Administration of the Agency

According to the FIA section 4(1)

The Superintendence of the Agency lies with the Federal Government as it is the body directly overlooking the FIA.

4 (2) The administration of the Agency shall vest in the Director General who shall exercise in respect of the Agency such of the powers of an Inspector General of Police under the Police Act, 1861 (V of 1861), as may be prescribed by rules.

ND12_8_2023

superintendence of agency can be done by

Where headquarters of Industrial Development Bank of Pakistan located?

The academy of ANF is situated in which city?

Correct Answer: Islamabad

 Anti Narcotics Force (ANF) was raised on 21 February 1995 by merging ANTF and PNCB.

PMC stands for

Correct Answer: Pakistan Medical Commission

16

Rangers headquarter in

Correct Answer: Islamabad
Show explanation
Explanation

Rangers headquarters are located in the following three cities.

Islamabad, ICT

Lahore, Punjab

Karachi, Sindh

***

PR stands for: Pakistan Rangers

Formed: 1942; 81 years ago (as Sindh Rifles)

Parent agency: Civil Armed Forces

The two corps are :

the Punjab Rangers (operating in Punjab province with headquarters in Lahore) 

the Sindh Rangers (operating in Sindh province with headquarters in Karachi).

 third corps headquarters in Islamabad but is only for units transferred from the other corps for duties in the federal capital.

ND-31-8--2023


17

When was Frontier Works Organization (WFO) established in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: 1966
Show explanation
Explanation

FWO: Frontier Works Organization

FWO: a military engineering organization

FWO established: 1966

FWO credited for construction:  bridges, roads, tunnels, airfields and dams in Pakistan

21

The head of supreme court is called ?

Correct Answer: chief justice

Explanation

22

Pakistan Narcotics Board (PNB) was set up in which year?

Correct Answer: 1957
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan Narcotics Board (PNB) was set up in 1957, in the Revenue Division in order to fulfill Pakistan's obligations under the International Opium Convention of 1925.

23

A madrasa for the study of teaching of Muslim Law in was established by the first Governor General of India in the year?

Correct Answer: 1781
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Warren Hastings was the first Governor General of India.
  • Warren Hastings established a madrasa in 1781.
  • Warren Hastings established a madrasa for the study of teaching of Muslim Law in Calcultta.
24

the steel mil was laid the foundation in----------with the aid of the USSR.

Correct Answer: 1973
Show explanation
Explanation

  • Pakistan Steel Mill was established in the reign of Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto
  • Pakistan Steel Mill was established with the cooperation of Russia
  • ****
  • Pakistan Steel Mill is located in Karachi, founded on 2 July 1973 with the support of Russia.
  • Headquarters of Pakistan Steel Mill Karachi, Pakistan.
  • Production Capacity: 1.1 Million Ton of Steel Expandable up to 3.0 Million Ton per annum.
25

“FIA” of Pakistan was established on ____________?

Correct Answer: 13 January, 1975
Show explanation
Explanation
  • FIA of Pakistan was established on January 13, 1975
  • ****
  • It was established under FIA Act, 1974 (Act-VIII of 1975) promulgated on 13-01-1975.
  • FIA stands for Federal Investigation Agency
  • ****

  • ****The operational structure of FIA****
  • Headquarters----> Islamabad, Pakistan
  • Agency executive-->Mohsin Hassan Butt Director-General
  • Parent agency------->Ministry of Interior
  • Major Units --------> 13

  • ***
  • FIA was established with the objective to deal with Smuggling, Narcotics, Currency offenses, Enforcement of Laws relating to Foreigners, Immigration & Passports, and offenses having inter-provincial ramifications.

26

Constitution of ANF is under:

Correct Answer: ANF act 1997
Show explanation
Explanation

There are the 17 Sections in ANF ACT 1997.

1 Short title and commencement

2 Definitions

3 Constitution of the Force

4 Superintendence and administration of the Force

5 Functions of the Force

6 Power of the members of the Force

7 Wearing of Uniform

8 Punishment

9 Grounds of punishment

10 Authority to award punishment

11 Terms of Service

12 Conduct and Discipline

13 Members to serve anywhere

14 Members as public prosecutors

15 Delegation of powers

16 Indemnity

17 Power to make rules

 

27

The term “Toshakhana” means___________?

Correct Answer: A department stores precious gifts received by officials
Show explanation
Explanation

The word Toshakhana is origin of: فارسی

Toshakhana means: treasure-house

Toshakhana, a government owned department (Pakistan)

Toshakhana department formed: 1974 (under the control of Cabinet Division)

The Toshakhana, the gift depository of Pakistan, was formed in ___________?

Correct Answer: 1974

The word Toshakhana is origin of: فارسی

Toshakhana means: treasure-house

Toshakhana, a government owned department (Pakistan)

Toshakhana department formed: 1974 (under the control of Cabinet Division)

29

Pakistan Coast Guard Act may be called?

Correct Answer: Pakistan Coast Guard Act, 1973
Show explanation
Explanation

PCG Formed in 1971.

PCG working, Pakistan Coast Guard Act, 1973.

PCG Headquarters located in Karachi, Sindh.

Pakistan Coast Guards (PCG) is a rare blend of sea and land based troops serving under one command.

The Coast Guards branch is distinguished from the Navy's Maritime Security Agency (MSA).

Pakistan Coast Guards (PCG) Headquarters located in?

Explanation

PCG working, Pakistan Coast Guard Act, 1973.

PCG Headquarters located in Karachi, Sindh.

Pakistan Coast Guards (PCG) is a rare blend of sea and land based troops serving under one command.

The Coast Guards branch is distinguished from the Navy's Maritime Security Agency (MSA).

Hazara University is located in

35

According to education authority of Pakistan ________seminaries are working in Pakistan

Correct Answer: 35000

Explanation

A seminary, school of theology, theological seminary, or divinity school is an educational institution for educating students in scripture, theology, generally to prepare them for ordination to serve as clergy, in academics, or mostly in Christian ministry.

When educational institutes closed due to covid-19

Explanation

38

Government performed by officers is called

Correct Answer: Bureaucracy
Show explanation
Explanation

Bureaucracy is a body of non-elected governing officials or an administrative policy-making group.

Historically, a bureaucracy was a government administration managed by departments staffed with non-elected officials.

Daily English Newspaper Dawn is based in the city of?

Explanation

Dawn was founded by Quaid-e-Azam.

Dawn is the largest and oldest English-language newspaper in Pakistan and the country's newspaper of record. 

Dawn headquarters: Karachi

In which year Sir Syed Ahmad Khan set up a school at Muradabad?

42

When was Pakistan Mineral Development Corporation (PMDC) set-up in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: 1974
Show explanation
Explanation

PMDC is an autonomous corporation under the administrative control of Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Resources.

PMDC founded in 1974.

43

Main functions of Pakistan Coast Guards (PCG) at Coastline are _____?

Correct Answer: All of these
Show explanation
Explanation

Main functions of Pakistan Coast Guards (PCG) at Coastline are.

  1. Special measures to effectively check smuggling of any kind across the frontiers of Pakistan in the Coastal Area of Arabian Sea
  2. enforcement of admiralty law in the international waters
  3. combat sea-based search and rescue missions
  4. The Pakistan Coast Guards are responsible for maintaining the constitutional law at the Pakistan's beaches and patrolling the riverine platforms while the combat sea-based search and rescue missions, enforcement of admiralty law in the international waters, and preventing the armed piracy falls under the responsibility 

پاکستان کوسٹ گارڈز (PCG) سمندر اور زمین پر مبنی فوجیوں کا ایک نادر امتزاج ہے جو ایک کمانڈ کے تحت خدمات انجام دے رہے ہیں۔ اس کے پاس اپنے انوینٹری کے جہاز اور سمندری سامان کے علاوہ زمینی سطح پر کام کرنے کے ذرائع ہیں۔ یہ انوکھا امتزاج زمین کے ساتھ ساتھ سمندر میں بھی آپریشن کرنے کی قوت اور صلاحیت کو قرض دیتا ہے۔ یہ غیر معمولی یونین پی سی جی کو دیگر سیکورٹی فورسز اور قانون نافذ کرنے والی ایجنسیوں کے درمیان فرق کرتی ہے جو ساحل کے ساتھ ساتھ کام کرتی ہیں، یا تو زمین پر یا سمندر میں۔

بحیرہ عرب کے ساحلی علاقے میں پاکستان کی سرحدوں سے کسی بھی قسم کی سمگلنگ کو مؤثر طریقے سے روکنے کے لیے خصوصی اقدامات

بین الاقوامی پانیوں میں ایڈمرلٹی قانون کا نفاذ

جنگی سمندر پر مبنی تلاش اور بچاؤ مشن

پاکستان کوسٹ گارڈز پاکستان کے ساحلوں پر آئینی قانون کو برقرار رکھنے اور دریا کے پلیٹ فارمز پر گشت کرنے کے ذمہ دار ہیں جبکہ جنگی سمندر پر مبنی تلاش اور بچاؤ مشن، بین الاقوامی پانیوں میں ایڈمرلٹی قانون کا نفاذ، اور مسلح بحری قزاقی کو روکنا ذمہ داری کے تحت آتا ہے۔

45

According to the Government of India Act 1935 India had been divided into:

Correct Answer: Eleven provinces
Show explanation
Explanation

The proposed all-India federation included 11 provinces of British India, 6 Chief Commissioners Provinces

The Government of India Act of 1935 was the last constitution of British India

The Government of India Act, of 1935 was an Act adapted from the Parliament of the United Kingdom.

It originally received royal assent in August 1935.

It was the longest Act of (the British) Parliament ever enacted until the Greater London Authority Act 1999 surpassed it.

ND27-7-2023

Who is the first Pakistani personality whose wax figure has been unveiled in Dubai?

Show explanation
Explanation

 A wax figure of former prime minister Benazir Bhutto will be unveiled at the Madame Tussauds museum in Dubai on 30 July 2023

 Benazir’s wax figure would be the first of a Pakistani personality at the museum’s Dubai location

Which organization has been given legal cover to screen government officials?

Correct Answer: ISI

Where headquarters of Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) located?

Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB)

52

Supreme Judicial Council shall consist of___________?

Correct Answer: All of above
Show explanation
Explanation

The Supreme Judicial Council is comprised of 5 members.

  • Chief Justice of Pakistan.
  • Two next most Senior judges of the supreme court.
  • Two most Senior Chief Justices of High Court.

****

The Supreme Judicial Council of Pakistan is a body of judges empowered under Article 209 of the constitution of Pakistan to hear cases of misconduct against judges.



53

FIA act means:

Correct Answer: Federal Investigation agency act
Show explanation
Explanation

The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) :

The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) is a border control, criminal investigation, counter-intelligence, and security agency of the Interior Secretary of Pakistan

Codified under the Constitution of Pakistan in 1974,

Formed: 13 January 1975

The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) has its headquarters in Islamabad.

Parent agency: Ministry of Interior

Major Units: 13

ND30-4-2023


54

Who can be deemed to be a public prosecutor?

Correct Answer: Both A & B
Show explanation
Explanation

Governor 

 The appointment of a public prosecutor and the administrative authority of the state were granted to the governor by Article 129 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan in 1973 and have the authority to appoint a public prosecutor, the governor.

59

Name of the border between India and Pakistan in Punjab?

Correct Answer: Radcliffe Line
Show explanation
Explanation

The name of international borderline between India and Pakistan is called the Radcliffe line,

the border line was drawn by a British officer Cyrill Radcliffe in 1947 and the same line became the border after partition in 1947.

 ***

Line of Control (LoC) is the De facto boundary between Indian-administered Kashmir and Pakistani-administered Kashmir.

Its current form was demarcated after the 1972 Simla Agreement.

60

Which range separate Balochistan Plateau from Afghanistan?

Correct Answer: Chaghi
Show explanation
Explanation

Chagai Hills is a range of granite hills in the Chagai District, Balochistan province.
61

India is located on the _______ side of Pakistan.

Correct Answer: East
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan is bordered by India to the east  

the Length of Pakistan is bordered by India is 3,323 kilometres (2,065 mi)

Pakistan is bordered by India Established on 17 August 1947

***

Pakistan is bounded by Iran to the west

while China borders the country in the northeast.

Afghanistan to the northwest and north

***

ND02-12-2022,05-12-2022

62

Who signed the Pak-Sino Boundary treaty 1963 from Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto
Show explanation
Explanation

Negotiations between the nations officially began on October 13, 1962, and resulted in an agreement being signed on 2 March 1963.

It was signed by foreign ministers Chen Yi for the Chinese and Zulfikar Ali Bhutto for the Pakistani.

The eastern tip of the Wakhan Corridor starts the Sino-Pak border between the People's Republic of China and Pakistan spanning about 559 km (347.3 mi).

Zulfikar Ali Bhutto, the Minister of External Affairs of Pakistan.

63

China, Sino-Pakistan Frontier Agreement was signed on ____?

Correct Answer: March 2, 1963
Show explanation
Explanation

This agreement was signed between Pakistan and China.

Chen Yi from China and Zulifqar Ali Bhutto from Pakistan signed this agreement.

64

Lahore share border with which Indian city?

Correct Answer: Amritsar
Show explanation
Explanation

Established 17 August 1947

Creation of the Radcliffe Line by Sir Cyril Radcliffe due to the Partition of Indiapak

65

Ganda Singh border is between:

Correct Answer: Ferozpur-Qasur
Show explanation
Explanation

The Pakistani village of Ganda Singh Wala served as the main border crossing between India and Pakistan in the 1960s & 70s.

It was closed to trade & transit in 1986 and the main border crossing was shifted north to Wagah.

66

When borders of Pakistan were drawn?

Correct Answer: 17 August, 1947
Show explanation
Explanation

The borders of Pakistan were drawn just after two days of partition that is on, 17 August 1947.

The border name is Radcliffe Line.

67

Which of the following state of India does not have border with Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Haryana
Show explanation
Explanation

The India-Pakistan border is also known as International Border.

The total length of the India-Pakistan border is 2,100 kilometers.

  1. Gujarat: 505 Km
  2. Punjab: 425 Km
  3. Rajasthan: 1,170 Km
68

Which of the following valley gifted to china by Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Shaksgam Valley
Show explanation
Explanation

The Shaksgam Valley was ceded to China by Pakistan in 1963 when both countries signed a boundary agreement to settle their border differences.

The agreement laid the foundation of Karakoram highway which was built jointly by the Chinese and Pakistani engineers in 1970s.

69

Karakorum Highway is built with the help of ----

Correct Answer: China
Show explanation
Explanation

 The Karakoram Highway was built by the governments of Pakistan and China.

It was started in 1959 and was completed and opened to the public in 1979.

Length: 1,300 km (810 mi)

Karakorum Highway is located in the Karakoram range

Karakorum highway in Pakistan starts from Hassan Abdal

Karakorum highway is also called N-35

****

nd9-2-2023



70

Multan metro bus project funded by_________?

Correct Answer: Punjab Government
Show explanation
Explanation

The Multan Metrobus, is a bus rapid transit  system in Multan, Punjab, Pakistan.

Construction on the line began in May 2015, while operations commenced on 24 January 2017.


71

Father of nuclear bomb of Pakistan name?

Correct Answer: A.Q.Khan
Show explanation
Explanation

Father of the nuclear bomb name A.Q khan

Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998 at the Chagai test site, in Balochistan.

5 Nuclear tests were conducted by Pakistan at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.

The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District of Balochistan Province.

Chagai-I was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

while 

Chagai II

Chagai-II is the codename assigned to the second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998

These tests followed the Chagai-I series

The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan

The number of tests is 1

Sh26-8-2023

72

Atomic Energy Programme for peaceful purposes was given a boost by Dr Usmani in?

Correct Answer: 1963
Show explanation
Explanation


Ishrat Usmani was a Pakistani nuclear physicist 

He chaired the Pakistan Atomic Energy Commission from 1960 to 1971.



73

Pakistan established its first Atom Energy Institute in:

Correct Answer: March 1956
Show explanation
Explanation

March 1956

In 1956, the Government of Pakistan established the Atomic Energy Commission (PAEC) to direct a new program. The United States handed Pakistan the first 5 MW Pakistan reactor (PARR-1) in 1962.

74

Which province got a women police station after 28 years in March 2022?

Correct Answer: Balochistan
Show explanation
Explanation

Inspector General Police (IGP) Balochistan Mohsin Hassan Butt inaugurated the first police station for women in Balochistan on March 4, 2022.

75

_________ branch to be headed by a Police Officer not below the rank of Assistant Inspector General to assist the Provincial Police Officer with the welfare of the Police and Police martyrs and their families.

Correct Answer: Welfare
Show explanation
Explanation

Welfare branch to be headed by a Police Officer not below the rank of Assistant Inspector General to assist the Provincial Police Officer with the welfare of the Police and Police martyrs and their families.

AGM 18-12-2022


76

A Deputy Superintendent of Police shall not be promoted unless he undergoes _________ course in a manner determined by the Provincial Police Officer.

Correct Answer: Junior Command
Show explanation
Explanation

A Deputy Superintendent of Police shall not be promoted unless he undergoes Junior Command course in a manner determined by the Provincial Police Officer.  

If you are an expert, let us know more details about this question please

AGM 18-12-2022


77

Which is the most Senior Police Rank?

Correct Answer: Inspector General
Show explanation
Explanation
List of Pakistan Police Rank with scale
Police Rank    Abbreviation    BPS  
Inspector General of Police IGP 22
Additional Inspector General  Addl: IG   21
Deputy Inspector General / Regional Police Officer / City Police Officer DIG / RPO / CCPO 20
Senior Superintendent of Police / Assistant Inspector General    SSP/AIG    19
Superintendent of Police    SP    18
Additional Superintendent of Police / Deputy Superintendent of Police  ASP / DSP  17
Police Inspector   Inspector  16
Sub-Inspector   SI    14
Assistant Sub-Inspector    ASI    11
Head Constable    HC    9
Constable    FC    07
78

_______ shall be responsible for maintaining law and order in the District.

Correct Answer: The head of District Police
Show explanation
Explanation

The head of District Police shall be responsible for maintaining law and order in the District.

If you are an expert, let us know more details about this question please



AGM 18-12-2022


79

Station House Officer means the:

Correct Answer: Officer incharge of a police station
Show explanation
Explanation

Station House Officer means incharge of a police station

The term of Office for Station House Officer shall not be less than one year and not more than two years unless transferred earlier due to exigency of service or misconduct. 

AGM 18-12-2022


80

Which is the most Junior Police Rank?

Correct Answer: Constable
Show explanation
Explanation
List of Pakistan Police Rank with scale
Police Rank    Abbreviation    BPS  
Inspector General of Police IGP 22
Additional Inspector General  Addl: IG   21
Deputy Inspector General / Regional Police Officer / City Police Officer DIG / RPO / CCPO 20
Senior Superintendent of Police / Assistant Inspector General    SSP/AIG    19
Superintendent of Police    SP    18
Additional Superintendent of Police / Deputy Superintendent of Police  ASP / DSP  17
Police Inspector   Inspector  16
Sub-Inspector   SI    14
Assistant Sub-Inspector    ASI    11
Head Constable    HC    9
Constable    FC    07

Which is the smallest unit of the police department?

Explanation

PTS stands for what?

The system of police in Mughal India was organized on the basis of _____________?

85

CPO stands for what?

Correct Answer: Central Police office

Explanation

86

Muhammad Bin Qasim died at the age of:

Correct Answer: 20
Show explanation
Explanation

Muhammad Bin Qasim attacked Sindh in 712 AD and died in 715 AD at the age of 20.

87

The Ali brothers launched Khilafat Movement against British in 1920. Names of Ali brothers were ________ ?

Correct Answer: Maulana Mauhammad Ali and Saukat Ali
Show explanation
Explanation

Maulana Mauhammad Ali and Saukat Ali, were the founders of Khilafat movement.

88

Which of these battle prove decisive between the Anglo-French rivalry in India?

Correct Answer: Battle of Wandiwash
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of Wandiwash was a battle in India between the French and the British in 1760.

89

Which party was formed by Asghar Khan?

Correct Answer: Tehrik-i-istaqlal
Show explanation
Explanation

Tehreek-e-Istiqlal  was a political party in Pakistan.

It was once the second most popular political party in Pakistan.

90

When British government shifted capital from Calcutta to Delhi______?

Correct Answer: 1911
Show explanation
Explanation

The capital of British India was transferred from Calcutta (now Kolkata) to Delhi in the year 1911.

The announcement to shift the capital was made during the Delhi Durbar  held in December 1911 to commemorate the coronation of King George V.


The new capital in Delhi was known as New Delhi and was designed by the architect Edwin Lutyens.

It became the center of British colonial administration in India.

91

During the early arrival of Britishers to India, who spend time with emperor Jehangir?

Correct Answer: Sir Thomsan Roe
Show explanation
Explanation

Sir Thomas Roe was an English diplomat of the Elizabethan and Jacobean periods.

He sat in the House of Commons at various times between 1614 and 1644.

92

Simon commission was rejected by?

Correct Answer: Both
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Simon's commission was rejected by Indian National Congress and  Muslim League
  • *
  • Simon Commission was appointed in 1928

  • It comprises 7 members of the British

  • It was opposed by both Jinnah & Gandhi
93

Court language during Ranjit Singh regime was___________?

Correct Answer: Persian
Show explanation
Explanation

Ranjit Singh's reign introduced reforms, modernisation, investment into infrastructure and general prosperity

94

In which of the following Act or reforms the dyarchy was abolished?

Correct Answer: India Act of 1935
Show explanation
Explanation

Diarchy is a form of government characterized by corule, with two people ruling a polity together either lawfully or de facto, by collusion and force.

95

When four women pilots Saba Khan, Nadia Gul, Mariam Halil and Saira Batool formally joined Pakistan Air Force?

Correct Answer: 30 March 2006
Show explanation
Explanation

Saba Khan, Nadia Gul, Mariam Halil and Saira Batool were first four female pilots to  formally joined Pakistan Air Force on 30 March 2006.

96

Federal Government deployed Army troops in all provinces after floods under article:

Correct Answer: Article 245
Show explanation
Explanation

In August 2022, the deployment of Army troops was approved under the article 245 of the constitution of Pakistan to protect the lives and properties of the people.

97

What is the rank of Force Commander of a regional directorate?

Correct Answer: Brigadier
Show explanation
Explanation

Brigadier is the rank of Force Commander of a regional directorate.

98

Newly selected Army chief Qamar Javed Bajwa belongs to Regiment___________?

Correct Answer: 16th Baloch Ragiment
Show explanation
Explanation

Bajwa was commissioned in 1980 in the 16th Battalion of the Baloch Regiment.

Commands: Chief of Army Staff 

Assumed office on 29 November 2016

______________________________________

Preceded by General Raheel Sharif

99

Who among the following is known as Kargil War Hero?

Correct Answer: Karnal Sher Khan
Show explanation
Explanation

Captain Karnal Sher Khan Shaheed Nishan-e-Haider was known as the hero of the Kargil war

Sher Khan was martyred on 5 July 1999 during the Kargil War between India & Pakistan.

Sher Khan was a captain in the 27th Sindh Regiment of the Pakistan Army 

Sher Khan was posted to the 12th NLI Regiment during the Kargil Conflict

Related Question to the Kargil War:

The Kargil War was fought between India and Pakistan on1999

Kargil War fought from the month of May to July 1999

The Kargil War is also known as the Kargil Conflict but in India, this conflict is known as Operation Vijay

Kargil's Issue was stopped by the intervention of the United States of America (USA).

Strength: India: 30,000 = Pakistan: 5,000

The Kargil War was fought in the Kargil district of Kashmir and elsewhere along the Line of Control (LoC).

General Pervez Musharraf was the Chief of the Army Staff of the Pakistani army during the Kargil War in 1999.

Gen VP Malik was the Chief of the Army Staff of the Indian army during the Kargil war in 1999.

ND15-10-2023


 

100

The highest non- operational Military award is?

Correct Answer: Sitar-e-Basalat
Show explanation
Explanation

Sitara-e-Basalat (Star of Good Conduct) is a non-operational gallantry award of Pakistan Armed Forces given to individuals for distinguished acts of gallantry, valor or courage while performing their duty.

It is given by President of Pakistan on the recommendation of service chief.

*******

VU CBT FIA SI ASI PAPER 13-12-2021

101

Navy Captain is equal in rank to army ____________?

Correct Answer: Colonel
Show explanation
Explanation

General is the highest rank of the Pakistan Army and it is recognized by one crossed baton, one crescent, and one star and also called a Four-Star General.

PAKISTAN NAVY RANKSPAKISTAN ARMY EQUIVALENT RANKSPAKISTAN AIR FORCE EQUIVALENT RANKS
CAPTAIN (Capt)COLONELGROUP CAPTAIN
COMMODORE (Cdre)BRIGADIERAIR COMMODORE
REAR ADMIRAL (RAdm)MAJOR GENERALAIR VICE MARSHAL
VICE ADMIRAL (VAdm)LIEUTENANT GENERALAIR MARSHAL


102

The name of Chief of Staff of Pak Army who died in uniform?

Correct Answer: b & c
Show explanation
Explanation

Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq was killed along with several of his top military officials and two American diplomats in a mysterious plane crash near Bahawalpur on 17 August 1988.


Asif Nawaz died on 8 January 1993, while he was jogging near his home in Rawalpindi.
103

When the post of commander-in-chief was converted into the post of Chief of Army Staff?

Correct Answer: 1972
Show explanation
Explanation

General Tikka Khan was the first chief of army staff from March 1972 until retiring on March 1976

Changed: 20 March 1972

From 1947 to 1972: The Commander-in-Chief of the Pakistan Army (reporting name: C-in-C) was normally the highest-ranking officer in the Pakistan Army.

104

First Chairman Joint chiefs of Staff Committee was___?

Correct Answer: General Muhammad Shariff
Show explanation
Explanation

The first Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee was General Muhammad Shariff of the Pakistan Army

He was appointed in March 1976. 


105

When was Constantinople was added in Muslim empire

Correct Answer: May 29 ,1453
Show explanation
Explanation

Shortly after ascending to Ottoman throne in 1415,Mehmand II began formulating plans for a major Assault on Constantinople 

106

Who built Charminar to commemorate the end of the plague?

Correct Answer: Sultan Muhammad Quli Qutab Shah
Show explanation
Explanation

The monument was built in 1591 by Muḥammad Qulī Quṭb Shah, as the first building in Hyderabad, his new capital.


107

Lord Dalhousie established ____________ department.

Correct Answer: Public Works
Show explanation
Explanation

Lord Dalhousie established Public Works Department in 1854.

108

The second meeting of Khilafat movement held in

Correct Answer: Amritsar
Show explanation
Explanation

The second Khilafat Conference was held in Amritsar in December 1919.

This conference was a significant event in the Khilafat Movement,


***

  1. First Khilafat Conference: Held in Bombay (now Mumbai) in July 1919.
  2. Second Khilafat Conference: Held in Delhi in November 1919.
  3. Third Khilafat Conference: Held in Amritsar in December 1919.
  4. Fourth Khilafat Conference: Held in Allahabad in July 1920.
  5. Fifth Khilafat Conference: Held in Calcutta (now Kolkata) in November 1920.
  6. Sixth Khilafat Conference: Held in Nagpur in December 1920.
  7. Seventh Khilafat Conference: Held in Karachi in February 1921.
  8. Eighth Khilafat Conference: Held in Delhi in November 1921.


109

When the Swadishi movement was started against the British?

Correct Answer: 7 August 1905
Show explanation
Explanation

Swadeshi movement was launched to reverse the: Partition of Bengal

110

Hindus started Swedishi Movement against ______________?

Correct Answer: British
Show explanation
Explanation

Swedishi Movement: 7 August 1905

Swadeshi movement was launched to reverse the: Partition of Bengal

111

In violation of the Salt Laws, Gandhiji started a movement called______________?

Correct Answer: Civil disobedience movement
Show explanation
Explanation

Civil disobedience is the active, professed refusal of a citizen to obey certain laws, demands, orders or commands of a government, corporation or other authority. By some definitions, civil disobedience has to be nonviolent to be called "civil".

112

Who was not in the favour of Civil Disobedience Movement?

Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
Show explanation
Explanation

Civil Disobedience movement was started by was started by Mahatma Gandhi.

113

When Sir Stafford Cripps announced his formula to seek the co-operation between the National Congress and Muslim League_________?

Correct Answer: March 30,1942
Show explanation
Explanation

The Cripps Mission was a failed attempt in March 1942 by the Indian government to secure full Indian cooperation and support for their efforts in World War II.

114

British settlers bought land in Bengal in order to:

Correct Answer: Cultivate indigo
Show explanation
Explanation

The poor farmers in Bengal took up indigo cultivation because they were forced to do so

115

Cabinet Mission was the ___________ effort of the British to keep India united.

Correct Answer: Last
Show explanation
Explanation

Cabinet mission arrived in India on March 24, 1946 sent by British government.

116

Who defeated the Marahthas in the third battle of Panipat_____?

Explanation

Fought: 1761

Location: Panipat

Result: Durrani victory

117

who built the mausoleum of Jahangir and where?

Correct Answer: Nur Jahan at Lahore
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Noor Jahan Tomb Lahore or Nur jahan (Maqbara) is a famous ancient mausoleum, positioned in Shahdara Bagh (lawn), Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan. It was constructed during in seventeenth Century.
  • in addition, this beautiful purple stone tomb changed into built for the Mughal Empress as her very last resting vicinity.
118

Pakistan resolution strengthen which theory ?

Correct Answer: Two nation Theory
Show explanation
Explanation

The two-nation theory was a concept that emphasised a separate state for the Muslims in the subcontinent.

Correct Answer: Khamnaur
Show explanation
Explanation

According to Abul Fazl, the decisive battle between Rana Pratap and Akbar was fought at Khamnaur.

120

How many volumes of the history of Akbar`s reign were written by Abul Fazl?

Correct Answer: Three
Show explanation
Explanation

Akbarnama first volume: deals with the birth of Akbar

Akbarnama second volume: describes the detailed history of the reign of Akbar till 1602

Akbarnama third volume: describes the administrative system of the Empire

 

121

Correct Answer: 13 years

Successor    Jahangir

ruling from 998 to 1030.

Died: April 30, 1030, Ghazni, Afghanistan

Correct Answer: Gujrat

Explanation

Razia sultana reigned India for how many years_________?

Correct Answer: Four
Show explanation
Explanation

Razia Sultana ruled India from: 1236 to 1240

125

‘Lakh Baksh’ was a title given to the ruler?

Correct Answer: Qutub-ud-din Aibak

Explanation

After Muhammad Ghuri's death, he started the first muslim dynasty in the subcontinent, called the Delhi Sultanate which was to rule for 320 years,

Died: November 14, 1210, Lahore

Ancient archaeological site Mohanjo Daro is located in the Civil district of Sindh:

Correct Answer: Larkana

Explanation

Mohenjo-Daro is located in Larkana District, Sindh, Pakistan,

in a central position between the Indus River and the Ghaggar-Hakra River.


127

Who among the following Sultan’s of Delhi assumed the title sikandar-i-sani?

Correct Answer: Alauddin khilji
Show explanation
Explanation

Alaud-Dīn Khaljī, born Ali Gurshasp, was a Turco-Afghan emperor of the Khalji dynasty that ruled the Delhi Sultanate in the Indian subcontinent

128

MAO school was established by:

Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan
Show explanation
Explanation
The Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College was founded by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in 1875
In 1877, the school was raised to college level.
Lord Lytton, the Viceroy of India at the time, laid the foundation stone of the MAO College on January 8, 1878.
The college was later upgraded to Aligarh Muslim University (AMU) in 1920.
129

What do you understand by the term "Kerb in foreign currency dealings?

Correct Answer: The exchange rate of currencies in the open market
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Kerb can represent both the unofficial stock market on which buying and selling operations are processed after marketplace closure, in addition to casual deals.
  • As a standard rule, such operations are processed through brokers on the order of customers when, for some purpose or other, their orders could not be processed at the stock market.
130

When Sir Syed Ahmed Khan start Ali Garh school?

Correct Answer: 1875
Show explanation
Explanation

Sir Syed Ahmed Khan start Ali Garh school in 1875

Ali Garh school convert into College 1877

Ali Garh college convert into University 1920

131

Which reforms conferred on the Muslims the double vote?

Correct Answer: Minto-Morley Reforms
Show explanation
Explanation

D

ANS:

In 1909 the Morley-Minto Reforms allowed isolated electorates to Muslims. In these the numerical quality of assembly chambers was expanded. 27 out of add up to 60 individuals were to be chosen and 5 seats were saved for the Muslims. In common government, Muslims were to be spoken to by isolated electorates.

132

The acceptance of demand for appointment of Muslim judges was also incorporated in:

Correct Answer: Minto-Morley Reforms
Show explanation
Explanation

A

ANS:

The Indian Councils Act 1909, known as the Morley-Minto Reforms (John Morley was the secretary of state for India, and Minto was viceroy)—gave Indians restricted parts within the central and common assemblies. Upper lesson Indians, wealthy landowners and businessmen were favored. The Muslim community was made a isolated voters and allowed twofold representation. The objectives were very traditionalist but they did development the elective rule.

133

After Sir Aga Khan, who was appointed president of Muslim League?

Correct Answer: Raja Sahib of Mahmoodabad
Show explanation
Explanation

C

ANS:

He was the President of the All-India Muslim League conferences held within the a long time 1917, 1918 and 1928 and as the President of British Indian Affiliation (Oudh Taluqdars) for the a long time 1917–21 and 1930–31

134

Who constructed five canals to remove scarcity of water___________?

Correct Answer: Feroz Shah Tughlaq
Show explanation
Explanation

Sultan Firoz Shah Tughlaq  was a Muslim ruler of the Tughlaq dynasty, who reigned over the Sultanate of Delhi from 1351 to 1388.

135

For how many years SOOMRO dynasty ruled in Sindh ?

Correct Answer: 402 years (1035 A.D – 1439 A.D)
Show explanation
Explanation

The Soomra dynasty was a kingdom of Sindh, and at times adjacent regions, located in what is now present-day Pakistan.

136

The saint who is also known as “Great Traveler” was____________?

Correct Answer: Jahanian Jahangasht
Show explanation
Explanation

Mīr Sayyid Jalāl ad-Dīn, better known as Jahāniyān Jahāngasht, was a Sufi saint from South Asia.

137

Pakistan’s citizenship act of ________ guarantees citizenship to anyone born in the country?

Correct Answer: 1951
Show explanation
Explanation

The Pakistan Citizenship Act of 1951 was enacted on 13 April 1951; stating the purpose of it is "to make provision for citizenship of Pakistan".

The Act has been amended several times, the last occurring in 2000.

The Act is divided into 23 sections.


138

When Liaquat Ali Khan became secretary of the All-India- Muslim League?

Correct Answer: 1936
Show explanation
Explanation

Answer:

B

 1936 

 He joined the Muslim Federation and became a member of the US State Legislative Council (1926–40). He is Jinna's right-hand man, especially as the Secretary of the All-India Muslim Federation (1936–47), and chaired the Central Parliamentary Commission of the Muslim Federation since 1945.

139

Second Session of the Muslim League was held on the residence of:

Correct Answer: Nawab Muzzamilullah
Show explanation
Explanation

A 

ANS:

The second normal session of Muslim League was held at Amritsar in 1908. The plot for sacred changes and partitioned electorates was talked about in this session.

140

Who was Lord Minto?

Correct Answer: Viceroy of India
Show explanation
Explanation

B

ANS:

After serving as Representative General of Canada from 1898 to 1904, Master Minto became the Viceroy of India in 1905, where he organizations numerous changes and carried on the conventions he had built up in Canada. He resigned to Britain in 1910 and, for his lifetime of benefit, was made a Knight of the Fastener.

141

Which reforms created awareness among Indian people?

Correct Answer: Minto-Morley Reforms
Show explanation
Explanation

A

ANS:

In 1909 the Morley-Minto Reforms allowed isolated electorates to Muslims. In these the numerical quality of assembly chambers was expanded. 27 out of add up to 60 individuals were to be chosen and 5 seats were saved for the Muslims. In common government, Muslims were to be spoken to by isolated electorates.

142

Which organization believed that Muslims should be eliminated from India?

Correct Answer: Hindu Mahasabha
Show explanation
Explanation

C

ANS:

Syama Prasad Mookerjee, a prominent pioneer of the Hindu Mahasabha, joined the Fazlul Haq service in Bengal, in 1941, as its back serve. A year some time recently that, Fazlul Haq, some time recently his aftermath with the Muslim Alliance, had moved the (in)famous determination, named as the ‘Pakistan resolution’, which committed the Muslim Association to a isolated Muslim country. For this, Haq had been upbraided by the Congress as a communalist.


143

The followers of Haji Shariat Ullah were known as:

Correct Answer: Faraizis

Explanation

144

Who is the author of the book titled "Muhammad Ali Jinnah Ambassador of Unity"?

Correct Answer: Sarojini Naidu

Explanation

Answer:

C

145

Sir John Marshal ordered the digging of Mohenjo-Daro in:

Correct Answer: 1922
Show explanation
Explanation

Sir John Hubert Marshall was the Director-General of the Archaeological Survey of India from 1902 to 1928. He oversaw the excavations of Harappa and Mohenjodaro, two of the main cities that comprise the Indus Valley Civilization. He ordered the digging of Mohenjo-Daro in 1922.

146

Which pact was conceived by Lord chemsford?

Correct Answer: Lukhnow pact
Show explanation
Explanation

Both Muslim and Congress participate in this pact in 1916

147

Which Muslim leader left politics, after the annulment of the partition of Bengal?

Correct Answer: Nawab Saleem ullah Khan
Show explanation
Explanation

Hindu community strongly opposed the partition of Bengal.

The partition of Bengal was announced on 20 July 1905 by Lord Curzon.

In December 1911, King George V at his Coronation Darbar in Delhi announced the revocation of the Partition of Bengal.

148

Mansabdari system was introduced by Mughal king

Correct Answer: Akbar
Show explanation
Explanation

The system was established by Emperor Akbar in the late 16th century.

Function: It created a structured system for appointments, promotions, and responsibilities within the nobility.


Each official (Mansabdar) was assigned a rank, which was based on two categories:

  • Zat (personal rank)
  • Sawar (cavalry rank).


149

Ahmad Shah Abdali launched his early invasions against_________?

Correct Answer: Marhattas
Show explanation
Explanation

 Mahrattas a member of the princely and military castes of the former Hindu kingdom of Mahpaarashtra in central India.

150

Mulana Ashraf Ali Thanvi, Maulana Rasheed Ahmad and Maulana Mahmood ul Hassan belonged to

Correct Answer: Deoband movement
Show explanation
Explanation

The aim of the Deoband Movement was moral and religious regeneration of the Muslim community.

151

Sher e Bangal was the title of which personality

Correct Answer: Fazal ul Haq
Show explanation
Explanation

A.K.Fazlul Huq full name Abul Kasem Fazlul Huq, also known as “Sher-e-Bangal.

He dedicated almost fifty precious years of his life to attaining separate nation for the Muslims of India.

152

Civil Disobedient movement was started by

Correct Answer: Mahamta Gandhi
Show explanation
Explanation

On March 12, 1930, Indian independence leader Mohandas Gandhi.

begins a defiant march to the sea in protest of the British monopoly on salt

His boldest act of civil disobedience yet against British rule in India

153

Who the PM of British at the time of partition of India?

Correct Answer: Attlee
Show explanation
Explanation

He remain in office 1945-51

Monarch: George 6

154

Who is the Pioneer of London Muslim League?

Correct Answer: Syed Ameer Ali
Show explanation
Explanation

155

Who rejected the verdicts of jurists that idol worshipers are liable to be murdered____________?

Correct Answer: Shamd-ud-Din Iltumish
Show explanation
Explanation

Shams ud-Din Iltutmish, was the third of the Mamluk kings who ruled the former Ghurid territories in northern India.

He was the first Muslim sovereign to rule from Delhi, and is thus considered the effective founder of the Delhi Sultanate.

156

Who initiated the cult of Shivaji against the Muslims of India________?.

Correct Answer: Bal Ganga dher tilak
Show explanation
Explanation

Tilak was the first leader of the Indian independence movement.

The British colonial authorities called him "The father of the Indian unrest."

157

The elections should be held on the basis of general adult franchise. This demand was made in____________?

Correct Answer: Lucknow pact
Show explanation
Explanation

The Lucknow Pact was an agreement reached between the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League (AIML) at a joint session of both the parties held in Lucknow in December 1916.

158

Why Syed Amir Ali resigned from the Muslim league in 1913 __________?

Correct Answer: The party had no clear cut policy
Show explanation
Explanation

Syed Ameer Ali Order of the Star of India was an Indian/British Indian jurist hailing from the state of Oudh from where his father moved and settled down at Bengal Presidency. 

159

Sindh was separated from Bombay presidency in the year __________ A.D.

Correct Answer: 1936
Show explanation
Explanation
  • ·         Syed, Sir Abdul Qayyum Khan was an  Indian Muslim leader
  • He also played their pivotal role, in Sindh separation from the Bombay Presidency in 1936.
  • ·      
  • ·         First Muslim General Mohammad Bin Qasim invaded Sindh
  • ·         The first Muslim League Government was formed in Sindh in 1943.
160

” If there were one hundred Ghandhis and two hundred Abdul Kalam Azads with the Muslim League but only one Muhammad Ali Jinnah with the Congress so that India would have never been divided” Who said this _____?

Correct Answer: Lakhshmee Pandit
Show explanation
Explanation

Vijaya Lakshmi Pandit was an Indian diplomat and politician.

First woman appointed to 6th Governor of Maharashtra.

8th President of the United Nations General Assembly.

161

On which date Indian troops moved into East Pakistan and began to advance toward Dacca.

Correct Answer: 16 November 1971
Show explanation
Explanation

ð  The war of 1971 between India and Pakistan lasted for 13 days from 3 December 1971 to the fall of Dacca (Dhaka) on 16 December 1971 .

ð  The war began with Operation Chengiz Khan's preemptive aerial strikes (india).

ð  on 16 December 1971 in Dhaka, marking the formation of East Pakistan as the new nation of Bangladesh.

ð  Officially, East Pakistan had earlier called for its secession from Pakistan on 26 March 1971.

 

162

Muhammad Ali Jinnah was the architect of:

Correct Answer: Lucknow Pact
Show explanation
Explanation

The Lucknow Pact is an agreement between:

  • the Indian National Congress headed by Maratha leader Bal Gangadhar Tilak) 
  • the All-India Muslim Federation led by Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

***

The Congress adopted it at its Lucknow session on December 29, 1916

And by the league on Dec. 31, 1916

163

The partnership Act was passed on:

Correct Answer: 8 April ,1932
Show explanation
Explanation

It extend to the whole of Pakistan

164

Pakistan took stands for Rohyngia muslims in :

Correct Answer: Myanmar

165

Shuddhi movement was started by ____.

Correct Answer: Swami Dayanand Saraswati
Show explanation

166

The British attitude for granting independence to India changed because of:

Towards the middle of the decade of 1940, the world war II was over and as a result of its impact, there was a wave of decolonization world over.

Which becomes first Pakistani film to be nominated at Cannes Film Festival?

Correct Answer: Joyland

170

who made the design of Pakistan's first stamp ticket?

Correct Answer: Abdul Rehman Chughtai
Show explanation
Explanation

Abdul Rehman Chughtai made the design of Pakistan's first stamp ticket

.. 

The first, ever postage stamp of Pakistan was issued on 9th July 1948 with the motif of the crescent and star with a leafy pattern.

The design of Pakistan's first stamp ticket was approved by Quaid-i-Azam.

Pakistan's first postal stamp was printed in October 1947


171

The highest point above sea level of Pakistan is?

Correct Answer: K-2
Show explanation
Explanation

K2 is the highest point in Pakistan. 

and second-highest mountain on Earth.

K-2 is Also Known As Godwin Austin

K2 mountain was first measured by Godwin Austen in 1856.

Godwin Austen was an English topographer (surveyor) 

'K' standing for Karakoram.

Mount K2 is part of the Karakorum Range located on the border of Pakistan and China.

K2 height 8,611 meters

It is located on the China–Pakistan border between Baltistan in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of northern Pakistan.

172

Which is the highest mountain of KPK?

Correct Answer: Tirich Mir
Show explanation
Explanation

Tirich Mir is the highest mountain of the Hindu Kush range, and the highest mountain in the world outside of the Himalayas–Karakoram range.
173

First Round Table Conference between Indian Politicians and the British government took place in the year?

Correct Answer: 1930
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The first Round Table Conference convened from 12 November 1930 to 19 January 1931

  • ****
  • The Second Round table conference was held from 7 September 1931 to 1 December 1931 in London. 
  • ****
  • The Third Round Table Conference was held in 1932 between November and December. 

  • ****

  • First round table conference: 1930

    2nd: 1931

    3rd:1932

    All in London

  • All three round table conferences were attended by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Tej Bahadur Sapru.
174

The first private hydel Power plant, constructed by Lariab Energy Limited is located at?

Correct Answer: Hub
Show explanation
Explanation

The Hub power station is the first and largest power station to be financed by the private sector in Southern Asia and one of the largest private power projects in the newly industrialized world.

 

175

Pakistan independence was announced first through which Radio station?

Correct Answer: Lahore
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan independence was announced first through Lahore Radio station.

Pakistan’s first radio station was setup in Karachi.

 

There are the following three radio stations existed at the time of independence of Pakistan on 14 August 1947.

1.      Peshawar Radio Station established in 1935

2.      Lahore radio station established in 1937

3.      Dhaka radio station established in 1939

******


176

The inaugural session of the first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was chaired by ___?

Correct Answer: J.N. Mandal
Show explanation
Explanation

    on 10th August 1947, first session of the first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was held at Sindh Assembly Building Karachi.

On 11th August 1947 Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was elected as the first Speaker/President of Pakistan.

On 11th August 1947 the National Flag was approved by the Assembly.

The inaugural session of the first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was chaired/chairman by Mr. J.N. Mandal

177

The inaugural meeting of the first constituent assembly was held on:

Correct Answer: 10th August 1947
Show explanation
Explanation

on 10th August 1947, first session of the first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was held at Sindh Assembly Building Karachi.


On 11th August 1947 Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was elected as the first Speaker/President of Pakistan.


On 11th August 194 the National Flag was approved by the Assembly.



The inaugural session of the first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was chaired/chairman by Mr. J.N. Mandal

Which country won the four-time hockey World Cup?

 Pakistanis the four-time champion: 

1971

1978

1982

1994.

Pakistan defeats Spain in the 1971 hockey world cup.

Pakistan beat India to secure the bronze medal at Hockey Champions Trophy 2012.

Total District in Pakistan 136

Facts about Pakistan Azla/Districts

 

2. How many districts in KPK?

35 (after 25th amendment 1973- FATA merge)

35✔

And 7 Divisions

30✔

And 7 Divisions

6. How many districts are in Azad Kashmir?

8. The smallest districts of Pakistan by area?

182

Name the only military ruler of Pakistan to be sentenced to death_?

Correct Answer: Pervez Musharraf
Show explanation
Explanation

Know everything about Musharraf

Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in 2001 until 2008.

Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

In June 2001, Musharraf became the president of Pakistan.

In 2006, Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire was published.

In March 2007, Musharraf suspended then chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry.

Musharraf imposed a state of emergency on November 3, 2007.

Resigned from his position of army chief November 2007

Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

Died: 5 February 2023

Dying place: Dubai, UAE

Disease: amyloidosis

Aged: 79

Buried: Karachi

 

 

183

Which Bank has given First loan in Millions to Pakistan government at the time independence?

Correct Answer: HBL Bank
Show explanation
Explanation

HBL Bank gave a loan to Pakistan for its first budget

***

HBL was the first commercial bank to be established in Pakistan in 1947

***

HBL moved its operations to Pakistan in 1947 at the request of Muhammad Ali Jinnah, hence becoming the first commercial bank to lay its foundation in the country

The first female leader who led a Muslim state was--------

Correct Answer: Benazir Bhutto
Show explanation
Explanation

Benazir Bhutto's first election to Prime Minister in 1988 made her the first woman to lead a Muslim-majority country

BB was Born on June 21, 1953, in Karachi

BB was Assassinated on December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi

BB Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007)

BB Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP)

BB Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977),

BB Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.

she served as Prime Minister of Pakistan from 1988 to 1990 and again from 1993 to 1996

She was the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan. 

****

Who is the first Hindu Lt Colonel in Pakistan Army?

Correct Answer: Kelash Kumar
Show explanation
Explanation

  • In 2019,  Kumar became the Pakistan Army's first Hindu Major

  • Kelash Kumar was posted in the Ministry of Defence.

    Kumar is from the Sindh province's Tharparkar area and is the country's first officer from the minority community to hold the position.

    Kelash Kumar was awarded the Tamaga-e-Dafa for spending 36 days at the world's highest post saddle in the Baltoro sector near K2

    He also received Tamga-e-Baqa and Tamga-e-Azam for his efforts in combating terrorism.

          187

          Pakistan's first Noble laureate, Dr. Abdus Salam died on:

          Correct Answer: 21 Nov 1996
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Dr. Abdus Salam was Died: Nov. 21, 1996

          Dr. Abdus Salam was Born in Jhan Punjab: Jan. 29, 1926

          Abdus Salam became the first Pakistani to win a Nobel Prize.

          n 1979,  Dr. Abdul Salam won the Nobel Prize for physics.

          Dr. Abdul Salam received Nobel Prize for combining two forces: Electromagnetic and weak forces

           He was the second Muslim scientist to get Nobel Prize.

          In 1952 he Ph.D. in theoretical physics from the University of Cambridge

          Awards

          Copley Medal: 1990

          Royal Medal: 1978

          Hughes Medal: 1964

          Pride of Performance: 1958

          Nishan-e-Imtiaz

          Adams Prize: 1958

          Lomonosov Gold Medal: 1983

          Atoms for Peace Award

          Gold Medal for Outstanding Contributions to Physics: 1981

          Smith's Prize for Essay: 1950

          Jožef Stefan Medal: 1980

          Cristoforo Colombo Prize: 1992

          ND06-08-2023

          192

          The word Pakistan is related to which language ?

          Correct Answer: Both A & B
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • the word Pakistan is related to the Persian and Urdu
          • **
          • ***
          • The word Pakistan consists of two parts, i.e., Pak and Istan.
          • While Pak is a Persian word,
          • the word Pakistan means a holy/ pure/ clean place (country) and not the 'Land of the Pure'
          •  the word Istan is from the word isthan, which is a Hindi word meaning a place.
          • ***

          • The word Pakistan was first introduced now or never in 1933 by Choudhry Rahmat Ali.

          Which is the First Book of Allama Iqbal?

          First Factory Act was passed during the viceroyship of which of the following?

          Correct Answer: Lord Ripon
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • First Factory Act was passed in the year 1881 during the viceroyship of Lord Ripon.
          • In the year 1885, a Factory Commission was appointed.
          • A Royal Labour Commission was appointed in the year 1891.
          • The main purpose of those commissions was to limit the working hours in the factory.

           

          200

          what is the meaning of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: holy land
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The holy land is the meaning of Pakistan
          • ***
          • The word Pakistan consists of two parts, i.e., Pak and Istan.
          • While Pak is a Persian word,
          • the word Pakistan means a holy/ pure/ clean place (country) and not the 'Land of the Pure'
          •  the word Istan is from the word isthan, which is a Hindi word meaning a place.
          • ***

          • The word Pakistan was firstly introduced in now or never in 1933 by Choudhry Rahmat Ali.

            Now or never was a pamphlet, referring to the names of the five northern regions of British India: Punjab, Afghanistan, Kashmir, Sindh and Baluchistan.

          223

          Which bank set Guinness World Record by installing world’s highest ATM at Khunjerab Pass

          Correct Answer: NBP
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The National Bank of Pakistan, (NBP) recently set a new “Guinness World Record” by installing the world’s highest ATM (automated teller machine) at the Pak-China border, Khunjerab Pass.

          The ATM has been installed at a height of 16,007 feet above sea level, making it world’s highest-altitude ATM anywhere in the world.

          This is the second time NBP has been awarded a position in Guinness World Record, the first time was in July 2009 for planting over 532,887 mangrove saplings in a day at Keti Bander.

          224

          The first bio-gas plant was installed in__________?

          Correct Answer: 1974
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The first biogas plant was installed in Mumbai, India, in 1859 at the Matunga leper asylum.

          It was the first anaerobic digestion-based biogas plant, utilizing human waste for lighting,

          What is the rank of Pakistan in the World Giving Index 2021?

          At the time of the partition of India, Kashmiris were derived into?

          The basis for the partition of India was the contention put forward in I940 by the Muslim League that.

          India's Muslims and Hindus constituted two separate nation

          The Kashmir conflict started after the partition of India in 1947 as both India and Pakistan claimed the entirety of the former princely state of Jammu and Kashmir

           The Kashmir conflict is also between China and India.

          •  India controls approximately 55% of the land area of the region 
          • Pakistan controls approximately 30% of the land area of the region.
          • China controls approximately 15% of the land area of the region

          Lowrai tunnel connect ________ with ________?

          Lowari tunnel connects Dir with Chitral,

          Lowari Tunnel  is a 10.4 kilometers (6.5 mi) vehicular tunnel under the Lowari Pass of the Hindu Kush mountain in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

          It is operated by the National Highway Authority and carries traffic on the N-45 National Highway,

          Its construction began in Sep 2015

          Its construction was completed in June 2017

          237

          ________ was the great Amnassadr Hindu-Musim Unity in the 1st phase of his Political career.

          Correct Answer: Quiad-e Azam
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Sarojni Naidu nominated Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah called the ambassador of the Hindu Muslim unity.

          History

          In December 1916, AIML and Congress met in Lucknow.

          It was due to untiring efforts of Jinnah that the Congress “agreed to separate electorate, for the first and the last time”.

          Due to these efforts of Jinnah, he was given the title of 'Ambassador of Hindu-Muslim unity' by Sarojni Naidu.

          River Indus extends to the Arabian Sea from:

          All important question "INDUS RIVER"

          The Indus River starts in Tibet

          The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar Lake

          The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

          The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

          River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

          The old name of the Indus River was: Sindhu

          National River of Pakistan: Indus River

          6 Barrages constructed in the Indus River

          Indus River Length: 3,180 km

          Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

          The upper Indus Plain ends at Mithankot

          NFC is a series of planned economic programs in Pakistan enacted since 1951.

          Simly dam is located at:

          Correct Answer: Islamabad

          Explanation

          Simly Dam east of Islamabad and Rawalpindi in Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan.

          Simly Dam is an eighty meters (260 toes) high earthen embankment dam on the Soan River, 30 kilometers (19 mi) 

          Simly Dam is the largest reservoir of consuming water for human beings dwelling in Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan.

          Planning for the dam began in 1962

          and it was not completed when expected in 1972 due to project delays.

          It was completed in 1983.

          17 to India

          and 5 to Pakistan.

          Pakistan was to receive 750 million rupees, which was initially delayed by the Indian Government.

          Which of following was the style of Mughal Architect ?

          Correct Answer: Indo-persian
          Show explanation

          Mughal buildings have a uniform pattern of structure and character, including large bulbous domes, slender minarets at the corners, massive halls, large vaulted gateways, and delicate ornamentation; examples of the style can be found in modern-day Afghanistan, Bangladesh, India and Pakistan.

          According to Pakistan Atomic Energy Commission, The Atomic blast done by Pakistan was measured on the Richter scale.

          a seismic signal of 5.0 on the Richter scale,

          with a total yield of up to 40 KT (equivalent TNT). Dr. A.Q.

          Chagai-I is the code name of five simultaneous underground nuclear tests conducted by Pakistan at 15:15 hrs PKT on 28 May 1998

          256

          How many nuclear tests did Pakistan conduct in 1998?

          Correct Answer: 5
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998 at the Chagai test site, in Balochistan.

          5 Nuclear tests were conducted by Pakistan at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.

          The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District of Balochistan Province.

          Chagai-I was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

          while 

          Chagai-II is the codename assigned to the second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998

          These tests followed the Chagai-I series

          The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan

          ND14-7-2023

          Khojak pass connects Chaman with _________-

          Location

          Balochistan

          Length

          3.9 KM

          Connects

          Qila Abdullah with Chaman

          Also known as

          Shela Bagh Tunnel

          Located

          West of Quetta

          Construction started

          1888

          Completed

          1891


          Note: The State Bank of Pakistan recognised the marvel of Khojak Tunnel and a picture of it was printed on the five-rupee note in 1976 which remained in circulation until 2005.


                     Important Passes of Pakistan

          1. Khyber Pass 

          connects: Peshawar to Kabul

          connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

          Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 

          **

          2. Bolan Pass 

          Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

          Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 

          **

          3. Lowari Pass 

          Connects: Chitral with Dir 

          Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 

          **

          4. Broghil Pass 

          Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

          Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 

          **

          5. Malakand Pass 

          Connects: Dir with Chitral 

          Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 

          ***

          6. Shandur pass

          Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

          Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 

          ***

          7. Khanjerab Pass (topmost in Pakistan) 

          Other name: Friendship Gate

          Connects: China with Pakistan 

          Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 

          ***

          8. Dorah pass

          Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

          Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 

          **

          9. Babusar Pass 

          Connects: Mansehra/Abbottabad with Gilgit

          **

          10. Kohat Pass

          Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 

          **

          11. Khojak Pass

          Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman

          **

          12. Tochi Pass

          Connects: Ghazni with Bannu

          **

          13. Karakar Pass

          Connects: Mingora with Buner 

          **

          and the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India

          Indus Waters Treaty, treaty, signed on September 19, 1960, between India and Pakistan

          The Treaty was signed at Karachi by Field Marshal Mohammad Ayub Khan,

          the then President of Pakistan, Jawaharlal Nehru

          The Treaty is effective from 1st April 1960

          Who translated objective resolution into urdu ?

          Prime Minister Khan, had presented it in the assembly on March 7, 1949.

          265

          Khilafat committee was formed in which city ?

          Correct Answer: Bombay
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The correct answer is Bombay in March 1919.

          The Khilafat Committee was formed in Bombay in March 1919.

          The Khilafat Movement, which began in 1919, brought the Muslims and the Hindus on a common platform against British rule, was the most important cause of the Non - Cooperation Movement.


          266

          Quaid e Azam said who is the show boy of congress ?

          Correct Answer: Abul Qalam Azad
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Azad had grown increasingly hostile to Jinnah, who had described him as the "Muslim Lord Haw-Haw" and a "Congress Showboy."

          Muslim League politicians accused Azad of allowing Muslims to be culturally and politically dominated by the Hindu community.


          267

          Who was the architect of Daccan Policy ?

          Correct Answer: Aurangezaib
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Deccan was incorporated into the Mughal Empire, and although previous emperors had policies for the Deccan, it was Emperor Aurangzeb's policies that would plant the seed of the Mughal Empire's decline.

          268

          Why did Quaid e Azam and Gandhi reject Cripps Mission?

          Correct Answer: Quit Indian Movement
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Cripps Mission was a failed attempt in late March 1942 

          The Cripps Mission was sent by the British government to India

            to obtain Indian cooperation for the British war efforts in the 2nd World War.

          The mission was headed by a senior minister Sir Stafford Cripps.

          the objective of Cripps Mission: To reform the constitutional issues

          After the Failure of the Cripps Mission, Mahatma Gandhi decided to launch his third major movement. This was the "Quit India" campaign

          On 8 August 1942, Mahatma Gandhi gave a clarion call to end British rule and launched the Quit Indian Movement at the session of the All-India Congress Committee In Mumbai

          ND5-07-2023

          The main three sectors of economy of Pakistan are agricultural, industrial and ______ sectors.

          Correct Answer: Services
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The main three sectors of economy of Pakistan are agricultural, industrial and services sectors.

          Textile is the largest export industry in Pakistan

          Agriculture is the largest sector of economy of Pakistan. 


          270

          Which city is called the thermal pole of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Jacobabad
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Jacobabad is called the thermal pole of Pakistan.

          Jacobabad is one of the biggest cities of Pakistan.

          Jacobabad is located in Sindh.

           

          271

          The main demand of Shimla Deputation which was accepted in the Minto-Morley Reforms of 1909 was about?

          Correct Answer: Separate Electorates
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Simla Deputation organized by the Indian Muslim leaders met the Governor-General and Viceroy Lord Minto in Simla on 1 October 1906. 

          The delegation of Simla deputation met Lord Minto on 1st October 1906

          Simla's deputation met with لارڈ منٹو

          شملہ وفد 1906

          1. شملہ وفد یکم اکتوبر 1906 کو وائسرائے ہند سے ملا.

          2. شملہ وفد کا صدر سر آغا خان تھا.

          3. اس میں 35 لوگ / ارکان شامل تھے.

          4. سب سے پہلے شملہ وفد ایم اے علی گڑھ کالج کے پرنسپل سے ملا.

          5. پر. نسپل نے سیکرٹری وائسرائے کرنل ڈونلپ سے یہ میٹنگ طے کر دیا، ان ہی کےذریعے وفد وائسرائے لارڈ منٹو سے ملا.

          6. چونکہ مسلمانوں کا یہ وفد وائسرائے ہند سے شملہ کے مقام پر ملا، اس وجہ سے اسکا نام شملہ وفد بن گیا.


          272

          Entities are required to comply with the following standards to operative their financial statements?

          Correct Answer: All of these
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          IAS (International Accounting Standards):


          • These are older accounting standards that were issued before IFRS.
          • Still valid unless replaced by IFRS.

          IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards):


          • Issued by the IASB (International Accounting Standards Board).
          • Modern framework used globally for preparing financial statements.

          ISA (International Standards on Auditing):


          • These are used by auditors, not for preparing financial statements but for auditing them.


          273

          Pakistan became a nuclear power on____?

          Correct Answer: 28 May 1998
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998 at the Chagai test site, Balochistan.

          5 Nuclear tests were conducted by Pakistan at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.

          The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District of Balochistan Province.

          Chagai-I was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

          ****

          nd9-2-2023

          274

          Who among the following political leaders drafted Wardah Scheme?

          Correct Answer: Zakir Hussain
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Zakir Husain Khan was an Indian economist and politician

          Zakir Husain Khan served as the third president of India, from 13 May 1967 until his death on 3 May 1969. 

          Zakir Husain Khan drafted the “Wardha Scheme” under the guidance of Gandhi

          Born: February 8, 1897, Hyderabad, India

          Died: May 3, 1969, New Delhi, India

          “Wardha Scheme”

          all India National Education Conference was held on October 22 and 23, 1937.

          The conference is called Wardha Educational Conference

          and the president of this conference was Gandhi himself.

          Free and compulsory education is to be provided for 7 years at a nationwide scale.

          The Queen's Proclamation incorporating the transfer of governance from East India Company to the British Crown was made public at Allahabad, on November 1, 1858 by Lord Canning the first Viceroy of India.

          325

          CPEC will link through which pass?

          Correct Answer: Khanjrab
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Karakar pass is located in: Hindu Kush, KPK

           Important Passes of Pakistan

          1. Khyber Pass 

          connects: Peshawar to Kabul

          connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

          Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


          2. Bolan Pass 

          Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

          Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


          3. Lowari Pass 

          Connects: Chitral with Dir 

          Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


          4. Broghil Pass 

          Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

          Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


          5. Malakand Pass 

          Connects: Dir with Chitral 

          Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


          6. Shandur pass

          Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

          Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


          7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

          Other name: Friendship Gate

          Connects: China with Pakistan 

          Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


          8. Dorah pass

          Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

          Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


          9. Babusar Pass 

          Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


          10. Kohat Pass

          Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


          11. Khojak Pass

          Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


          12. Tochi Pass

          Connects: Ghazni with Bannu


          13. Karakar Pass

          Connects: Mingora with Buner

          333

          The first Population Census was held in Pakistan in the year?

          Correct Answer: 1951
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          There have been only 6 nationwide censuses.

          1. First Census was held in Pakistan in 1951.
          2. The second Census was held in Pakistan in 1961.
          3. The third Census was held in Pakistan in 1972.
          4. The fourth Census was held in Pakistan in 1981.
          5. The fifth Census was held in Pakistan in 1998.
          6. The Sixth Census was held in Pakistan in 2017.
          7. The seventh census was held in Pakistan in 2023


          Additional Information:

          Census has been undertaken every 10 years

          it was beginning in 1872 under British viceroy Lord Mayo
