            The Prime minister of Pakistan is Shehbaz Sharif Trending

            The capital of Pakistan is: Islamabad

            the President of Pakistan is: Arif Alvi

            the Currency of Pakistan is: the Pakistani rupee


            ***




            339

            Gandhara Civilization is the attribute of?

            Correct Answer: Taxila
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Taxila was considered as Pearl of Gandhara Civilization. 
            • language of the Gandhara civilization was:  Prakrit
            • ***
            • Gandhara's language was a Prakrit or "Middle Indo-Aryan" dialect, usually called Gāndhārī.
            • Hindko from Peshawar, which was the capital of Gandhara, came from Shuraseni Prakrit a language spoken in Gandhara.

            • AGM 30.4.23


            340

            Which of following was the contemporary of Eight Indian ruler ?

            Correct Answer: Ameer Khusro
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             Amīr Khusrau, was an Indian Sufi singer, musician, poet and scholar who lived during the period of the Delhi Sultanate.

            Born: 1253, Patiali, India

            Died: October 1325, Delhi, India

            Place of burial: New Delhi, India

            341

            During British Administration in which year Shimla declared as Summer Capital of British India ?

            Correct Answer: 1864
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In 1864 Shimla was declared as the summer capital of India.


            342

            During the Khilafat movement Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar advised Muslim to migrate to ?

            Correct Answer: Afghanistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Muhammad Ali Jauhar , was an Indian Muslim activist, prominent member of the All-India Muslim League, Indian National Congress, journalist and a poet, a leading figure of the Khilafat Movement and one of the founders of Jamia Millia Islamia.

            343

            Who was the founder of Bahawalpur state ?

            Correct Answer: Nawab Bahawal
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The princely state of Bahawalpur was founded in 1802 by Nawab Mohammad Bahawal Khan II

            after the break-up of the Durrani Empire, and was based in the city.

            344

            In which movement claimed land belongs to Allah no one can levied tax and rule ?

            Correct Answer: Farazi movement
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Faraizi movement was started and led by Haji Shariatullah in 1819.

            The movement extolled Muslims of Bengal to give up un-Islamic practices and act upon their duties as Muslims.

            The Faraizi movement was widely received in the districts of Dhaka, Faridpur, Barisal, Mymensingh and Comilla.


            345

            How many non-Muslim seats are in the National assembly?

            Correct Answer: 10
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The National Assembly of the parliament consists of 336 members old was 342 reducing KPK from 61 to 55.

            It become 342 after 26th amendment

            National Assembly is the lower  house of parliament 

            Senate is the Upper house of parliament 


            NamePunjabSindhKPKBaluchistanICTTotal
            General1416145163266
            Women3214104-60
            Non-Muslims-----10
            Total1737555203336

            According to Article 51 (1) and 3(a) of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan

            Article 51. (1) There shall be [three hundred and thirty-six] seats for members in the National Assembly, including seats reserved for women and non-Muslims.

            Reference Click

            Armughan i Hijaz was written by ________?

            349

            Who is the youngest-ever Nobel Prize Laureate?

            Correct Answer: Malala Yousafzai
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Malala Yousafzai is the youngest-ever Nobel Prize Laureate.

            Malala Born: 1997

            She was attacked: 2012

            She is: the second Pakistani to receiver a Nobel Prize.

            350

            How many constitutions have been enforced in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Three
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            First Constitution

            1956 Constitution contained 234 articles divided into 13 parts and 06 schedules.

            Second Constitution

            1962 Constitution contained 250 articles divided into 12 parts and 03 schedules.


            Third Constitution

            1973 Constitution contained 280 articles and 07 schedules with Objective Resolution.

            The Constitution consists of 280 articles,

            12 chapters and

            a schedule divided into 7 chapters

            Effective date: 14 August 1973

            Drafted by the Government of Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto

            351

            The Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973, was promulgated on:

            Correct Answer: 14 August ,1973
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             the 1973 constitution of Pakistan was enacted on August 14, 1973


            Zulifqar             Ali Bhutto introduced a new constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.


            According to the 1973 constitution, both the national assembly and senate elect the president of Pakistan

            whereas the prime minister is selected by the national assembly.


            The Bicameral Legislature system was introduced for the first time in Pakistan in the constitution of 1973.


            Khan Abdul Wali Khan was the opposition of the National Assembly at the time of promulgation.

            ND29-5-2023

            352

            Which organization conducts competitive examination for federal civil services in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: FPSC
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            FPSC conducts competitive examination for federal civil services in Pakistan.

            FPSC stands for Federal Public Service Commission.


            353

            Pakistan has decided to mark which date as Black Day?

            Correct Answer: May 9
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            On 16 May 2023, National Security Committee decided to observe May 9 — when multiple incidents of violence and vandalism were reported amid protests sparked by PTI chief Imran Khan’s arrest — as “Black Day” at the national level.

            354

            If population of Gilgit and Azad Kashmir is also taken in account then by population Pakistan is world's __________ largest country.

            Correct Answer: Fifth
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan is the world's fifth-most populous country

            The population density in Pakistan is 287 per Km2 (742 people per mi2).

            Pakistan world's second-largest Muslim population.

            the top 10 most populous countries in the world

            1. China
            2. India
            3. the United States
            4. Indonesia
            5. Pakistan
            6. Brazil
            7. Nigeria
            8. Bangladesh
            9. Russia
            10. and Mexico



            355

            Which country offered access to Pakistani ports in March 2021?

            Correct Answer: Uzbekistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Uzbekistan, which currently relies on Iranian seaport of Bandar Abbas for external trade, is exploring other options and is prioritizing Pakistani ports because of short distance, being more economical and due to some political considerations.

            Uzbekistan is working with Pakistan on the development of two options — the first is the Trans-Afghan railway project while the second is the road route via China.

            the last copy was sold on the 15th of January 1980.

            358

            Number of Prime Ministers of Pakistan till 2022?

            Correct Answer: 23
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Since its independence on 14th August 1947, Pakistan has seen 23 Prime Ministers.

            However, none of them has completed five-year tenure.

            Liaquat Ali Khan, the first Prime Minister of Pakistan, is the longest-serving Prime Minister in Pakistan. 

            Mian Muhammad Shahbaz Sharif has been appointed as the 23rd Prime Minister of Pakistan

            List of Prime Ministers of Pakistan 

             Term#   Name

            2              Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin

            3              Mohammad Ali Bogra

            359

            Who is the current chairman of the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB)?

            Correct Answer: Syed Mohsin Naqvi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Appointing date: 6 February, 2024

            Tenue: three-year term

            Replaced: Zaka Ashraf

            ****


            Updated & Verified on 14 August 2025



            360

            Blind Dolphins are found in the river of:

            Correct Answer: Ganges River
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Blind Dolphins are found in Indus river in Pakistan and Ganges river in India and Bangladesh.

            ***********

            The Indus River dolphin, also known as the “bhulan,” is one of the world's most endangered cetaceans. 

            It is endemic to the Indus River Basin of Pakistan and northwestern India. 

            Blind Dolphins are found in the lower parts of the Indus River in Pakistan and in River Beas, a tributary of the Indus River in Punjab, India.


            ND20-6-2023





            361

            Blink dolphins found from which river in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Indus River
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Blind Dolphins are found in the Indus River in Pakistan and Ganges River in India and Bangladesh.

            ***********

            The Indus River dolphin, also known as the “bhulan,” is one of the world's most endangered cetaceans. 

            It is endemic to the Indus River Basin of Pakistan and northwestern India. 

            Blind Dolphins are found in the lower parts of the Indus River in Pakistan and in River Beas, a tributary of the Indus River in Punjab, India.

            ND20-6-2023

            362

            Who is the first female Poet of Balochistan?

            Correct Answer: Rabbia Khuzdari
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Rabia Khuzdari is the first female Poet of Balochistan.

            Rabia Balkhi, also known as Rabia al-Quzdari (or Khuzdari)

            Rabia Khuzdari was a 10th-century writer who composed poetry in Persian, Arabic, and Balochi. 

            Rabia Khuzdari is the first known female poet to write in Persian.

            Rabia Khuzdari is known as 'Magas Roeen' in her verses. 

            while 

            Gul Khan Nasir is the national poet of Balochistan.

            The Balochistan Sufi poet Mast Tawakali 

            Sayad Zahoor Shah Hashemi is considered the Father of Balochi.

            366

            PPP stands for

            Correct Answer: Pakistan Peoples Party

            The Pakistan People's Party is a center-left, social-democratic political party in Pakistan.

            It is currently the second-largest party in the Senate

            Pakistan Peoples Party was launched by Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto.

            The party was founded on November 30, 1967, in Lahore

            Senate: 20 / 100

            Gilgit-Baltistan Assembly: 5 / 33

            Azad Kashmir Assembly: 13 / 53

            پاکستان کے پہلے صدرسکندر موزا کہاں دفن ہیں؟

            Correct Answer: ایران
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            صدر پاکستان محمد یحیٰ خان نے ان کی میت پاکستان لانے اور یہاں دفن کرنے سے صاف انکار کر دیا

            ایرانی بادشاہ محمد رضا شاہ پہلوی نے خصوصی طیارے کے ذریعے اسکندر مرزا کی میت تہران لانے کا حکم دیا یہاں سرکاری اعزاز کے ساتھ ان کی آخری رسومات ادا کی گئیں۔

            Who became the first transgender doctor in the history of of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Sarah Gill
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             For the first time in the history of Pakistan, a transgender person was conferred a Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) degree in Karachi. 

            Sarah Gill, a transgender person, made history by becoming the country’s first transgender doctor.


            377

            The Geneva Pact was signed on April 14, 1988 between Pakistan and ______ ?

            Correct Answer: Afghanistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Geneva Accords, known formally as the agreements on the settlement of the situation relating to Afghanistan,

            were signed on 14 April 1988 at the Geneva headquarters of the United Nations,

            between Afghanistan and Pakistan,

            with the United States and the Soviet Union serving as guarantors. 


            378

            How many were the members of Basic Democracies from East Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: 40000
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            After the imposition of Martial Law in 1958,

            General Muhammad Ayub Khan introduced the concept of basic democracy through the Basic Democracy Order, in 1959.

            On 26 October, Ayub Khan announces his system of 80,000 Basic Democrats

            The voting was to be indirect, as the President had to be elected by the 80,000 "basic democrats", who made up the Electoral College.

            The electoral system was made indirect, and the `Basic Democrats', for both wings were declared Electoral College for the purpose of electing the Assemblies and the President.

            (40,000 from each East & West Pakistan).

            ND23-4-2023



            379

            Nadir Shah invaded India in __ :

            Correct Answer: 1739
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • 1739
            • Emperor Nader Shah, the Shah of Persia (1736–47) and the founder of the Iranian Afsharid dynasty of Persia, invaded Northern India, in the end attacking Delhi in March 1739.
            • His navy had easily defeated the Mughals at the battle of Karnal and could ultimately capture the Mughal capital inside the aftermath of the warfare.
            380

            Garam Chashma is located in which province which got international recognition by UNESCO?

            Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Two of the scenic tourist spots in KPK received international recognition as Biosphere Reserves by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organisation (UNESCO).

            One of the designated areas is Garam Chashma in Chitral, and the other extends from Bagnoter to Barian in Abbottabad district.

             

            381

            The desert in the Bahawalpur district in Punjab is known as ________ desert.

            Correct Answer: Cholistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Cholistan Desert is located 30 kilometers from Bahawalpur, Punjab.

            Locally this desert is known as Rohi.

            Jeep Rally is also held in Cholistan Desert. 

            Cholistan lies on the southern part of the Punjab.

            398

            In which Division Malir , Karachi East, Karachi Central and Karachi West are counted?

            Correct Answer: Karachi South
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Karachi district has been de-merged into its 5 original constituent districts namely Karachi East, Karachi West, Karachi Central, Karachi South and Malir. Recently Korangi has been upgraded to the status of sixth district of Karachi. 

            399

            The area around the river is known as _______?

            Correct Answer: Bela
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Area around a river is called Bela

            Area between two rivers is called Doab      دوآب (دو+آب)

            400

            Jamia Millia Islamia was established on?

            Correct Answer: 1920
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Jamia Millia Islamia, an institution originally established at Aligarh in United Provinces, India in 1920. It became a Central University by an act of the Indian Parliament in 1988.

            401

            Which city is on the border of Sindh and Punjab Provinces?

            Correct Answer: Kashmore
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Kashmore District is a part of Larkana Division.

            Its old name was Khizmor.

            Its border is connected with Punjab and Balochistan provinces.
            402

            Who was the first Woman Governor of Gilgit Baltistan?

            Correct Answer: Begum Shama Khalid
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Shama Khalid from Gilgit-Baltistan who served as the 2nd Governor of the Gilgit Baltistan
            403

            In which year the Broad Peak was first climbed?

            Correct Answer: 1957
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Broad Peak  is a mountain in the Karakoram on the border of Pakistan and China,

            the 12th highest mountain in the world at 8,047 metres (26,401 ft) above sea level.

            It was first ascended in June 1957 by Fritz Wintersteller, Marcus Schmuck, Kurt Diemberger, and Hermann Buhl of an Austrian expedition.
            404

            In which city Military College Jhelum is located?

            Correct Answer: Sarai Alam Gir
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Military College Jhelum (MCJ) is a feeder military high school, located at Sarai Alamgir , District Jhelum but now in Gujrat District, Pakistan.

            Military College Jhelum (MCJ) Established in 03 March 1922.
            405

            How many Peaks in Pakistan are higher than 7000 meters?

            Correct Answer: 108
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan has five above 8000 meter peaks out of the fourteen including K-2 (8611m).

            108 Peaks in Pakistan are higher than 7000 meters
            406

            Kotli is the famous city of ____?

            Correct Answer: AJ&K
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Kotli is a city in Kotli District of Azad Kashmir,

            It lies on the Poonch River

             
            407

            Shah Jo Risalo is the best-known collection of romantic poetry in the ____ Language.

            Correct Answer: Sindhi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Shah Jo Risalo is a poetic compendium of famous Sindhi Sufi poet Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai

            Language: Sindhi

            Subject: Sufism Culture History

            The Risalo was first published in 1866 by the German philologist Ernest Trumpp.

            It contains thirty chapters, called Sur.

            408

            Who made design of Minar e Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Nasreddin Murat-Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Minar e Pakistan constriction start.

            March 23, 1960

            Minar e Pakistan Completed

            October 31, 1968 (8 years)

            What is the old name of Minar e Pakistan?

            Manto Park

            How many steps are there in Minar e Pakistan?

            Four steps

            How tall is Minar e Pakistan?

            196 ft. 8 inches (70 meters)
            409

            Who was the first Prime Minister of Pakistan hanged to death?

            Correct Answer: Zulfikar Ali Bhutto
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Zulfikar Ali Bhutto served as the 9th Prime Minister of Pakistan from 1973 to 1977,

            Zulfikar Ali Bhutto served as the fourth President of Pakistan from 1971 to 1973.

            Zulfikar Ali Bhutto Died April 4, 1979, Adiala Jail

            410

            What is the height of Sakasar peak?

            Correct Answer: 1500 meters
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Sakesar is the highest mountain in the Salt Range in Pothohar in Pakistan
            Elevation             1,552 m
            411

            Who Built the Lahore?

            Correct Answer: Akbar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Akbar the Great held his Court In Lahore for 14 years from 1584 to 1598, and built the Lahore Fort, as well as the city walls which had 12 gates. 

            412

            Where is the famous "Shangrila" lake and resort situated in Northern Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Skurdu
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Lower Kachura Lake, also known as Shangrila Lake is located in Kachura village in Skardu city at a height of 2,500 metres.

            The lake is also known as Shangrila lake after a resort built on its bank in 1983.

            413

            The upper Indus platue consist of province of:

            Correct Answer: Punjab
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Indus is a transboundary river of Asia and a trans-Himalayan river of South and East Asia.

            414

            MQM Boycott election in which year?

            Correct Answer: 1993
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Leader Altaf Hussain

            Founder Altaf Hussain

            Founded March 18, 1984

            415

            Zulifkar Ali Bhutto was hanged on:

            Correct Answer: 4 April 1979
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Zulfikar Ali Bhutto was a Pakistani barrister and politician who served as the 9th Prime Minister of Pakistan from 1973 to 1977, and prior to that as the fourth President of Pakistan from 1971 to 1973.

            416

            Al-khair university is the famous university of ______

            Correct Answer: AJK
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Al-Khair University is  located in Azad Kashmir,
            • It holds its main campus in the town of Bhimber.
            • Sanctioned by Pakistan's Higher Education Commission.
            • Founded by Dr. Muhammad Bashir Goraya
            • Private university
            • Founded in 1994

            Last updated question by NA on 12-JUNE-2026

            417

            Which of these is the longest Bazzar in Pakistan and Asia ?

            Correct Answer: Batkhela
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Batkhela is the main tehsil, city and capital of Malakand District, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

            418

            What is the length of Khaber pass

            Correct Answer: 53 km
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Khyber Pass is a mountain pass in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province of Pakistan, on the border with Afghanistan.

            Length: 53 km

            Location: Between Landi Kotal and Jamrud

            Range: Hindu Kush

            419

            What is the name of largest city of Swat valley

            Correct Answer: Mingora
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Mingora is the largest city of Swat Valley

            Gwalerai is a famous city for apples in Swat

            KNOWN ABOUT SWAT

            Swat District also known as the Swat Valley, is a district in the Malakand Division of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan

            Swat was merged with Pakistan in 1849

            On 28 July 1969, General Yahya Khan announced the full integration states of Chitral, Dir, and Swat.

            Swat is located in the Hindu Kush mountain range in KPK  

            Swat is the 15th-largest district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

            Capital: Saidu Sharif

            Swat is called the Switzerland of Pakistan because of its snow-capped mountains and beautiful scenery.

            ND26-9-2023

            420

            Which of these fort is in Gilgit Baltistan?

            Correct Answer: Altit Fort
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Altit Fort is an ancient fort at Altit town in the Hunza Valley in Gilgit Baltistan, Pakistan.

             carried the title of 'Mir'

            Altit Fort and in particular the Shikari Tower is around 1100 years old

            The fort received the UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Award for Cultural Heritage Conservation in 2011.

            Altit Fort Restored: 2007

            ND-8-08-2023

            421

            Naran valley is located in which province of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: KPK
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Naran is a medium-sized town in upper Kaghan Valley in Mansehra District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province of Pakistan.

            422

            Which city is called city of lakes?

            Correct Answer: Skardu
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Lake Manchar is the largest freshwater lake in Pakistan.

            It is located west of the Indus River in Sindh.

            Karambar Lake is the deepest lake.

            423

            Dr. Abdul Salam received Nobel Prize on combining what two forces?

            Correct Answer: Electromagnetic and weak forces
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Dr. Abdul Salam received Nobel Prize for combining two forces: Electromagnetic and weak forces

            In 1979,  Dr. Abdul Salam won the Nobel Prize for physics.

            He was the second Muslim scientist to get Nobel Prize.

            Abdus Salam became the first Pakistani to win a Nobel Prize.

            Dr. Abdus Salam was Born in Jhan Punjab: Jan. 29, 1926

            Dr. Abdus Salam was Died: Nov. 21, 1996

            In 1952  Ph.D. in theoretical physics from the University of Cambridge

            Awards

            Copley Medal: 1990

            Royal Medal: 1978

            Hughes Medal: 1964

            Pride of Performance: 1958

            Nishan-e-Imtiaz

            Adams Prize: 1958

            Lomonosov Gold Medal: 1983

            Atoms for Peace Award

            Gold Medal for Outstanding Contributions to Physics: 1981

            Smith's Prize for Essay: 1950

            Jožef Stefan Medal: 1980

            Cristoforo Colombo Prize: 1992

            ND5-07-2023 

            424

            Which lake emerged in the Hunza valley due to an Earthquake?

            Correct Answer: Attabad
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Attabad Lake  is a lake located in the Gojal region of Hunza Valley in Gilgit−Baltistan, Pakistan.

            It was created in January 2010 as the result of a major landslide in Attabad.

            425

            Chapal Kabab originates from which province ?

            Correct Answer: Kpk
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Khyber Pakhtunkhwa often abbreviated as KPK and formerly known as the North-West Frontier Province, is one of the four provinces of Pakistan.

            426

            Skardu city is located at the junction of Indus river and __ river

            Correct Answer: Sigar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Skardu  is a city located in Gilgit−Baltistan, Pakistan, and serves as the capital of the Skardu District.

            Skardu is situated at the confluence of the Indus and Shigar Rivers.

            The Indus River running through the region separates the Karakoram from the Himalayas

            427

            What is the other name of Shangrila lake near Kachura village ?

            Correct Answer: Kachura Lake
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Lower Kachura Lake is located in Kachura village in Skardu city at a height of 2,500 metres.

            The lake is also known as Shangrila lake after a resort built on its bank in 1983.

            428

            Which civilian award is attributed with Imran Khan as Cricketer?

            Correct Answer: Hilal e Imtiaz
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In 1992, Khan's team won the Cricket World Cup.

             For this achievement, Khan received the Hilal-e-Imtiaz, the second highest civilian award.

            429

            Forests and rangelands support 30 million herds of livestock. How much amount this livestock contributes to Pakistan’s annual export earnings ?

            Correct Answer: $400 million
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            جنگلات اور رینج لینڈز 30 ملین ریوڑ کی مدد کرتے ہیں۔ یہ مویشی پاکستان کی سالانہ برآمدی آمدنی میں 400ملین ڈالر کا حصہ ڈالتا ہے


            430

            Geographical area of Pakistan is _____ million hectare?

            Correct Answer: 79.6
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Out of the total area of 79.6 million hectares,

            22.1 million hectares are cultivated.

            The rest (57.5) of the territory is comprised of cultural waste, densely populated forests and rangelands.

            431

            The Book named "Causes of the Indian revolt" is written by

            Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Syed Ahmed Khan was one of the leading figures of Muslim India, and the Revolt of 1857 was the defining moment of his life.

            He wrote this analysis soon after he witnessed the brutal revenge the British took on Delhi once the Revolt was suppressed

            432

            The book 'Exit West' is written by which Pakistani novelist

            Correct Answer: Mohsin Hamid
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Mohsin Hamid is a Pakistani novelist, writer and brand consultant.

            His novels are Moth Smoke, The Reluctant Fundamentalist, How to Get Filthy Rich in pRising Asia, and Exit West.

            433

            In which region is the famous peak "Tao Butt " situated in Pakistan

            Correct Answer: AJK
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            the famous peak "Tao Butt " situated in AJK

            434

            River Sutlej rises in Tibet (China) and meets the Indus. Its length is ____ kms.

            Correct Answer: 1,370
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Origin of Sutlej river is Mount Kailash

            It is the easternmost tributary of the Indus River.

            435

            How many rivers are found in Muzaffarabad ?

            Correct Answer: 3
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Muzaffarabad is the capital and largest city of Azad Kashmir, and the 60th largest in Pakistan.

             The district is bounded by the Pakistani province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa in the west, the Kupwara and Baramulla districts of Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir in the east, and the Neelum District in the north.

            436

            Where is canal road situated in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Lahore
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Canal Bank Road is a major eight lane east-west signal free road which extends along the banks of the Lahore Canal in Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan.

            437

            Zhob valley is located in which province of Pakistan

            Correct Answer: Balochistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Zhob  formerly known as Fort Sandeman or Appozai, is a city in Balochistan..

            Zhob is located on the banks of Zhob River .

            438

            What is the village on the Indian side of Wahga Border called ?

            Correct Answer: Attari
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The border crossing draws its name from Wahga village, near which the Radcliffe Line, the boundary demarcation line dividing India and Pakistan upon the Partition of British India, was drawn.

            At the time of the independence in 1947, migrants from India entered Pakistan through this border crossing.

            439

            Harnoi lake is in which city

            Correct Answer: Abbottabad
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Harnoi Abbottabad is a famous picnic spot, with freezing waterfalls

            440

            Author of the book "Tehqeeq-ul-Hind" was ___

            Correct Answer: Al Biruni
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Al-Biruni was an Iranian scholar and polymath from Khwarezmi a region which encompasses modern-day western Uzbekistan, and northern Turkmenistan.

            441

            Swilk lake is in which city ?

            Correct Answer: Kalar Kahar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Swaik Lake is a lake located in tehsil Kallar Kahar, Chakwal District .

            442

            What is another name of Indus river

            Correct Answer: Sindh
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Indus is a transboundary river of Asia and a trans-Himalayan river of South Asia. The 3,180 km river rises in Western Tibet, flows northwest through the Ladakh and Gilgit-Baltistan regions of Kashmir

            Indus River Length: 3,180 km

            Indus River Mouths: Arabian Sea, Rann of Kutch, Nalsarovar bird sanctuary

            Indus River Tributaries: Sutlej River, Chenab River, Jhelum River, Ravi River,

            Indus River Sources: Tibetan Plateau, Senge Zangbu, Gar River

            Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

            443

            Kinds of forest in Pakistan are coniferous forest, irrigated plantation riverain forests and ________ ?

            Correct Answer: Scrub forests
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Three Kinds of forest in Pakistan are:

            1. coniferous forest,
            2. irrigated plantation riverain forests
            3. Scrub forests
            444

            Chitral town is at the feet of which mountain?

            Correct Answer: Tirchmir
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Chitral is a town situated on the Chitral River in northern Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

            Chitral served as the capital of the Chitral princely state that encompassed the region until its direct incorporation into West Pakistan in 1969. 

            Area: 57 km²

            445

            The author of "The Millat and Menace of Indianism" is

            Correct Answer: Ch. Rehmat Ali
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Pakistan National Movement was founded in 1933 by Choudhry Rahmat Ali who is also credited with creating the name "Pakistan", for a separate Muslim homeland in South Asia.

            His famous pamphlet NO AND NEVER
            446

            Proposed Kala Bagh dam is ?

            Correct Answer: Hydro
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The proposed site for the dam is situated at Kalabagh, in Mianwali district of Punjab. 


            447

            What is the name of second largest salt mine in the world which is located in Pakistan ?

            Correct Answer: Khewra
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Khewra Salt Mine is located in Khewra, north Jhelum District, Punjab, Pakistan, which rises from the Indo-Gangetic Plain.

            It is Pakistan’s largest and oldest salt mine and the world’s second-largest mine.

            Khewra Salt Mine another name is Mayo Salt Mine

            448

            Where is Mushakpuri mountain situated in Pakistan ?

            Correct Answer: Nathiagali
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Mukeshpuri is a 2,800-metre-high mountain in the Nathia Gali Hills, in the Abbottabad District of the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

            It is 90 kilometres north of Islamabad.

            449

            What is the famous shopping spot in Muree?

            Correct Answer: Mall Road
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Murree is a mountain resort town, located in the Galyat region of the Pir Panjal Range.

            District Rawalpindi Tehsil Muree

            It has average altitude of 2,291 metres

            450

            Which of these forts is not in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Red fort
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Delhi in India that served as the main residence of the Mughal Emperors.



            Red Fort, also called Lal Qalʿah, also spelled Lal Kila or Lal Qila, Mughal fort in Old Delhi, India.
            It was built by Shah Jahān in the mid-17th century and remains a major tourist attraction.
            451

            Which of these is the largest zoo of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Lahore Zoo
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Lahore Zoo in Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan, established in 1872.

            largest zoo of Pakistan

            452

            Which of these mosques is not in Pakistan ?

            Correct Answer: Hagia Sophia
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hagia Sophia, officially the Holy Hagia Sophia Grand Mosque, and formerly the Church of Hagia Sophia, is a Late Antique place of worship in Istanbul, designed by the Greek geometers Isidore of Miletus and Anthemius of Tralles

            453

            Pir Sohawa tourist resort is located in which hill

            Correct Answer: Margalla
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pir Sohawa is a rapidly developing tourist resort located 17 kilometers  from Islamabad on top of Margalla Hills.

            454

            How many tunnels in Mangla Dam?

            Correct Answer: 5
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Mangla Dam is situated on the Jhelum River 

            It is the seventh-largest dam in the world.

            Mangla Dam constructed on River Jhelum is to the help of the World Bank is located in Kashmir

            Mangra Dam is a multipurpose dam on the  Jhelum River in the Milpool district of Pakistan.

            Mangra Dam is named after the nearby village of Mangra.

            Construction was started in 1961 and it was completed in 1967.

            Mangla Dam has 5 tunnels and a power station

            ND17-7-2023

            455

            When was Muzaffaeabad made capital of AJK?

            Correct Answer: 1947
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            After the 1948-49 war, Muzaffarabad was made the capital of Azad Jammu and Kashmir.

            It is the largest city and the capital of Azad Kashmir

            Muzaffarabad was founded in 1646 by Sultan Muzaffar Khan


            456

            Simli Dam was built on river ?

            Correct Answer: Soan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Simly Dam is an 80 m high earthen embankment dam located in northeast of Islamabad and Rawalpindi in Rawalpindi District, Punjab.

            Constructed in 1983.

            457

            Which province of Pakistan has most forest

            Correct Answer: KPK
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Peshawar is the capital of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and its largest city. It is the sixth-largest in Pakistan.

            458

            The appointment of Quaid e Azam as Governor General of Pakistan was made through?

            Correct Answer: Nomination
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The ouath of Quaid e Azam  was taken  by Justice  Abdul rasheed 

            459

            Which is the oldest cantonment of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Sialkot Cantt
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Sialkot Cantonment is the oldest cantonment.

            Sialkot Cantonment was established in 1852 by the British Indian Army.

            As gleaned from the old records, the Sialkot Cantonment is the precursor of Wazirabad Garrison which stood established for a sojourn from 1849-52. The site of Sialkot Cantonment was chosen by Sir Charles Napier, GCB, the then Commander-in-Chief, in 1851.

            460

            Pakistan has won how many medals in the Paralympic Games?

            Correct Answer: 3
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            SH9-8-2023

            461

            Which head-work irrigation system were unlawfully given to India

            Correct Answer: Both
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Madhopur Headworks is a barrage on the Ravi River in the Indian state of Punjab.

            It is located in the Pathankot district on the border with Jammu and Kashmir. 

            The headworks was one of the first irrigation projects constructed in Punjab during the British Raj, within 10 years of the conquest of Punjab

            First built: 1875

            Length: 2,700 feet (820 m)

            Latest built: 1959

            ***

            Ferozepur Head Works was constructed under the Sutluj Velly project during the year 1926

            ND14-7-2023

            462

            White turban is a prominent part of traditional dress of which province__?

            Correct Answer: Balochistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The clothing of the people of Baluchistan, Pakistan consists of various styles of kameez and shalwar, turban, shoes and head scarfs.

            463

            What is the Gilgit Baltistan's overcoat called____?

            Correct Answer: Shuqa
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Shuqa is the most distinct winter clothing of the region of GB.

            Historically it has its roots in mountainous region of Tajikistan, Pamir, Badakhshan and Iran

            464

            Lungi Shalwar is a part of which provinces dress of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Balochistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             Shalwar refers to loose trousers and kameez refers to shirts.

            Baluchistan got the status of a province on 1st July 1970

            Baluchistan province is located in South West of Pakistan.

            Baluchistan covers 42 percent of the area of Pakistan

            Capital of Baluchistan is Quetta.

            The area of Baluchistan is 3,47,190 Km2.

            Baluchistan is the biggest province of Pakistan by area

            Balochistan was given the status of a province during the era of Yahiha Khan


            465

            Who was the first president of India after independence (Indian National) ?

            Correct Answer: Rajendra Parsad
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In-office from 1950 to 1962.

            First PM Of India

            Jawaharlal Nehru

            466

            In which year Ali Garh Trusteeship bill was passed______?

            Correct Answer: 1889
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In year 1889, Ali Garh Trusteeship bill was passed.

            Syed Ahmed Khan started the Aligarh movement for the educational upliftment of the Muslim community.

            In 1875, Sir Syed Ahmed Khan founded the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College in Aligarh

            Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College  Got Status Of College 1877  

            Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College got the Status Of a University in 1920


            467

            The name of first Pakistan made sub Marine is____?

            Correct Answer: Augusta 90B
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan Navy has only two submarines on active duty.

            Three of the five Agosta class submarines it has are either being upgraded or have defects

            468

            General Zia dissolve Assembly assembly according to which article ?

            Correct Answer: 58 2 (b)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            On 29 May 1988, Prime Minister Junejo was dismissed by President Zia who leveled charges on incompetency and economic stagflation and immediately called for new general elections. 

            469

            According to Which report suggested federal government in India?

            Correct Answer: Nehru Report
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Nehru Report of 28 August 1928 was a memorandum to appeal for a new dominion status and a federal set-up of government for the constitution of India.

            It also proposed for the Joint Electorates with reservation of seats for minorities in the legislatures.

            470

            Who was Dada bhai Naoroji ?

            Correct Answer: All
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Dadabhai Naoroji also known as the "Grand Old Man of India" and "Unofficial Ambassador of India" was an Indian political leader, merchant, scholar and writer

            471

            What is the national dress of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Shlwar Qameez
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The shalwar kameez is national dress of Pakistan and is worn by men and women in all Five provinces Punjab, Sindh, Balochistan, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa And Gilgit-Baltistan in the country and in Azad Kashmir.

            472

            President and Prime Minister must be Muslim in which constitution of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: 1973
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            An important Islamic provisions declared that no law shall be enacted which is repugnant to the injunctions of Islam as laid down in the holy Quran and the Sunnah and that existing laws shall be brought into conformity within injunction of Islam as laid down in the holy Quran and Sunnah.

            473

            Rattanbai Jinnah died at the age of---

            Correct Answer: 29 years
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            Rattanbai Jinnah  was the wife of Muhammad Ali Jinnah.
            Born: February 20, 1900, Mumbai, India
            Died: February 20, 1929, London, United Kingdom

            474

            Naryab Dam is in ?

            Correct Answer: KPK
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Small Dam is located in Naryab, Tehsil Thal.

            It is one of a number of small water reservoir to provide water for irrigation and drinking in times of drought.

            There are no other dam within 55 km². pak

            475

            Prime Minister Imran Khan on _________ inaugurated “the world’s biggest” Miyawaki urban forest in Lahore:

            Correct Answer: 9 August 2021
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The forest has been developed on over 100 kanals having a total of 165,000 plants that will grow 10 times quicker than in a normal forest owing to the unique Miyawaki technique.

            476

            Which city of Pakistan means " The forest in Sky "

            Correct Answer: Shugran
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Shogran means “Forest in the sky”.

            Some of the lofty peaks from here, which are ” Moosa Ka Musalla ” and ” Makra “having height of 14000 feet, and Malika-e-Parbat.

            Shogran is a serene resort in the Kaghan Valley. 

            477

            In which month, bus service started between Srinagar and Islamabad

            Correct Answer: April 2005
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The bus runs a distance of 170 kilometres (110 mi).

            The bus service was officially launched on April 7, 2005 and was flagged-off by the Indian Prime Minister Dr. Manmohan Singh.

            478

            Which Indian PM Initiated Indo Pakistan peace dialogue on Kashmir in 2004

            Correct Answer: A.B Vajpayee
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            479

            Which leader presented the Chenab Formula to resolve Kashmir Dispute

            Correct Answer: Abdul Qayoom Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            the Chenab formula presented by Abdul Qayoom Khan  

            He founded this formula on April 16, 2021 

            It is one of the seven popular formulas 


            This series of formulas ranges to two extremes:

            • allotting the entire Jammu and Kashmir state to Pakistan
            • allows the entire state to India

            NA21-8-2023

            480

            Which of the following is the highest court of a province?

            Correct Answer: High Court
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The first High Court was established in 1866 in the sub-continent.

            There are five High Courts in Pakistan. 

            481

            The total area of ICT is?

            Correct Answer: 906 sq.km
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            ICT stands for Islamabad Capital Territory
            Islamabad area:             906.5 km²

            482

            Chaman border is located in which province of Pakistan ?

            Correct Answer: Baluchistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             It is situated just south of the West–Chaman border crossing with the neighboring Kandahar Province of Afghanistan.

            483

            The area around rivers is called:

            Correct Answer: Bela
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The land between two rivers is called Doab.

            484

            Pakistan’s plains are divided into

            Correct Answer: 2
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Indus River plain is a vast expanse of fertile land, covering about 200,000 square milea, with a gentle slope from the Himalayan piedmont in the north to the Arabian Sea in the south.

            485

            In which year Ojhri Camp Incident occurred

            Correct Answer: 1988
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Ojhri Camp  was a military storage center located in Rawalpindi Military District in Rawalpindi.

            On 10 April 1988 Ojhri Camp disaster.


            486

            Tolinton market is located in which city?

            Correct Answer: Lahore
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Tollinton Market, as one crosses the old Anarkali Bazaar to one's right, out emerges the Lahore City Heritage Museum - a single storied structure, reminiscent of Anglo-Indian architecture, which vividly breaks the monotony of the large, red brick buildings located adjacent and opposite to it on Mall Road.

            487

            Who among these was not a member of vital signs band

            Correct Answer: Ali Zafar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Vital Signs were a Pakistani pop and rock band formed in Rawalpindi in 1986 by two Peshawar University students.

            488

            Which of these is a part of Pakistani national dress

            Correct Answer: Kurta
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The shalwar kameez is national dress of Pakistan and is worn by men and women in all provinces of Pakistab.

            Shalwar refers to loose trousers and kameez refers to shirts.

            489

            What is Sindhi and Balochi mirror embroidery called ?

            Correct Answer: Sheesha
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             Shisheh  or mirror-work, is a type of embroidery which attaches small pieces of mirrors or reflective metal to fabric.

            490

            What is an other name of Jinnah's cap

            Correct Answer: Karakul cap
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            A karakul  also called a Uzbek hat is a hat made from the fur of the Qaraqul breed of sheep.

            491

            Which colored dressing people usually do on mehendi ceremony

            Correct Answer: Yellow
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The predominant colours are mostly yellow, orange and red, but with changing times and trends.

            492

            Sindhi topi is a part of which cultures dress?

            Correct Answer: Sindh
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Together with Ajrak or Saraiki Ajrak, the Sindhi cap is regarded as an essential part of Sindhi and Saraiki culture.

            493

            Ahmad Shah Pitras Bukhari was a famous writer of which language?

            Correct Answer: Urdu
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Syed Ahmed Shah, commonly known as Patras Bokhari, was a Pakistani humorist, writer, broadcaster, and diplomat

            Born: October 1, 1898, Peshawar

            Died: December 5, 1958, New York, New York, United States

            Books: Patras Kay Mazameen

            Full name: Pir Syed Ahmed Shah Bokhari

            Awards: Hilal-i-Imtiaz

            Previous office: Permanent Representative of Pakistan to the United Nations (1951–1954)

            ND17-7-2023

            494

            What is the Language spoken in Muzaffarabad?

            Correct Answer: Kashmiri
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Muzaffarabad is the capital and largest city of Azad Kashmir, and the 60th largest in Pakistan.

             The district is bounded by the Pakistani province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa in the west, the Kupwara and Baramulla districts of Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir in the east, and the Neelum District in the north.

            495

            The main Aim of Home Rule movement was :

            Correct Answer: To attain self government within the British empire
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The primary objective of the Home Rule Movement was to achieve self-government within the British Empire through the use of constitutional means. 

            496

            In ___ English was made the medium of instruction in Subcontinent.

            Correct Answer: 1835
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            On this day in 1835, Lord Macaulay successfully westernised education in India; English was made the official language for the government and courts, and was adopted as the official medium of instruction.

            497

            Which city is famous for Kashikari in Pakistan

            Correct Answer: Multan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Kashi Kari- meaning mosaic art in Persian-is a form of decorative art that involves ceramic assortment on tiles, faience and fabric.

            498

            What is the name given to a famous lake in Gilgit which was formed after an earthquake?

            Correct Answer: Attabad
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Attabad Lake located in the Gojal region of Hunza Valley in Gilgit−Baltistan, Pakistan.

            It was created in January 2010 as the result of a major landslide in Attabad.

            499

            Where is Pakistan National Council of Arts located in Pakistan ?

            Correct Answer: Islamabad
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            PNCA- Pakistan National Council of Arts is the country's first national art gallery.

            It opened to the public on Sunday, August 26, 2007.

            500

            Dhoti or Lungi is usually associated with which provincial culture of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Punjab
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The shalwar kameez is national dress of Pakistan and is worn by men and women in all Five provinces Punjab, Sindh, Balochistan, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa And Gilgit-Baltistan in the country and in Azad Kashmir. 

            Nice work getting this far. Consistency beats intensity — come back tomorrow and keep stacking sets.
